⚡

Cloud Functions Overview

Basic

Cloud Functions is Google's serverless execution environment for building and connecting cloud services. Write simple, single-purpose functions that are attached to events emitted from your cloud infrastructure and services.

9
Runtimes
Supported languages
1000
Concurrent
Executions per function
540s
Timeout
Maximum execution time
0
Cold Starts
With min instances
⚡ Key Capabilities
  • Event-Driven: Trigger functions from HTTP requests, Cloud Storage changes, Pub/Sub messages
  • Auto-Scaling: Automatically scales from zero to handle any load
  • Pay-per-Use: Only pay for the compute time you consume
  • Multi-Language: Support for Node.js, Python, Go, Java, .NET, Ruby, and PHP
🔧

Runtimes & Supported Languages

Basic

Cloud Functions supports multiple runtimes and programming languages, allowing you to write functions in the language that best fits your needs and existing codebase.

📄
Node.js
JavaScript/TypeScript runtime with npm package support
🐍
Python
Python runtime with pip package management
🔵
Go
Compiled Go runtime for high performance
☕
Java
JVM-based runtime with Maven/Gradle support
🔷
.NET
C# and F# runtime with NuGet packages
💎
Ruby
Ruby runtime with Bundler gem management
Node.js: HTTP Function Example
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2
3// Register an HTTP function with the Functions Framework
4functions.http('helloHttp', (req, res) => {
5 const name = req.query.name || req.body.name || 'World';
6
7 res.status(200).send(`Hello ${name}!`);
8
9 // Log the request for debugging
10 console.log(`Request received for: ${name}`);
11});
12
13// Export for testing
14module.exports = {
15 helloHttp: functions.http('helloHttp', (req, res) => {
16 const name = req.query.name || req.body.name || 'World';
17 res.status(200).send(`Hello ${name}!`);
18 })
19};
python
1from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
2import functions_framework
3
4app = Flask(__name__)
5
6# HTTP function using Functions Framework
7@functions_framework.http
8def hello_http(request):
9 """HTTP Cloud Function.
10 Args:
11 request (flask.Request): The request object.
12 Returns:
13 The response text, or any set of values that can be turned into a
14 Response object using `make_response`
15 """
16 request_json = request.get_json(silent=True)
17 request_args = request.args
18
19 if request_json and 'name' in request_json:
20 name = request_json['name']
21 elif request_args and 'name' in request_args:
22 name = request_args['name']
23 else:
24 name = 'World'
25
26 return f'Hello {name}!'
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "fmt"
5 "net/http"
6)
7
8// HelloHTTP is an HTTP Cloud Function with a request parameter.
9func HelloHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
10 name := r.URL.Query().Get("name")
11 if name == "" {
12 name = "World"
13 }
14
15 fmt.Fprintf(w, "Hello %s!\n", name)
16}
Python: Cloud Storage Trigger Function
python
1import functions_framework
2from google.cloud import storage
3
4# Triggered by a change to a Cloud Storage bucket
5@functions_framework.cloud_event
6def hello_gcs(cloud_event):
7 """Cloud Function triggered by Cloud Storage."""
8 data = cloud_event.data
9
10 event_id = cloud_event["id"]
11 event_type = cloud_event["type"]
12
13 bucket = data["bucket"]
14 name = data["name"]
15 metageneration = data["metageneration"]
16 time_created = data["timeCreated"]
17 updated = data["updated"]
18
19 print(f"Event ID: {event_id}")
20 print(f"Event type: {event_type}")
21 print(f"Bucket: {bucket}")
22 print(f"File: {name}")
23 print(f"Metageneration: {metageneration}")
24 print(f"Created: {time_created}")
25 print(f"Updated: {updated}")
26
27 return f"File {name} processed successfully"
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Storage} = require('@google-cloud/storage');
3
4// Instantiates a client
5const storage = new Storage();
6
7// Triggered by a change to a Cloud Storage bucket
8functions.cloudEvent('helloGCS', cloudEvent => {
9 console.log(`Event ID: ${cloudEvent.id}`);
10 console.log(`Event type: ${cloudEvent.type}`);
11
12 const file = cloudEvent.data;
13 console.log(`Bucket: ${file.bucket}`);
14 console.log(`File: ${file.name}`);
15 console.log(`Metageneration: ${file.metageneration}`);
16 console.log(`Created: ${file.timeCreated}`);
17 console.log(`Updated: ${file.updated}`);
18
19 return `File ${file.name} processed successfully`;
20});
🌐

HTTP Functions

Intermediate

HTTP functions handle web requests and provide RESTful APIs. They support various HTTP methods, request parsing, response formatting, and middleware patterns for robust web applications.

HTTP Function Examples
python
1from flask import Flask, request, jsonify, make_response
2from functools import wraps
3import jwt
4import hashlib
5import hmac
6from google.cloud import functions_framework
7
8app = Flask(__name__)
9SECRET_KEY = 'your-secret-key'
10
11# Authentication middleware
12def require_auth(f):
13 @wraps(f)
14 def decorated_function(*args, **kwargs):
15 auth_header = request.headers.get('Authorization')
16 if not auth_header or not auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):
17 return jsonify({'error': 'Missing or invalid token'}), 401
18
19 token = auth_header.split(' ')[1]
20 try:
21 jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=['HS256'])
22 except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
23 return jsonify({'error': 'Token expired'}), 401
24 except jwt.InvalidTokenError:
25 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid token'}), 401
26
27 return f(*args, **kwargs)
28 return decorated_function
29
30# CORS middleware
31@app.after_request
32def add_cors_headers(response):
33 response.headers['Access-Control-Allow-Origin'] = '*'
34 response.headers['Access-Control-Allow-Methods'] = 'GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS'
35 response.headers['Access-Control-Allow-Headers'] = 'Content-Type, Authorization'
36 return response
37
38# RESTful API endpoints
39@app.route('/api/users', methods=['GET'])
40def get_users():
41 """Get all users with pagination and filtering."""
42 page = int(request.args.get('page', 1))
43 limit = int(request.args.get('limit', 10))
44 search = request.args.get('search', '')
45
46 # Mock data - in real app, fetch from database
47 users = [
48 {'id': 1, 'name': 'Alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
49 {'id': 2, 'name': 'Bob', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
50 {'id': 3, 'name': 'Charlie', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'}
51 ]
52
53 # Apply search filter
54 if search:
55 users = [u for u in users if search.lower() in u['name'].lower() or search.lower() in u['email'].lower()]
56
57 # Apply pagination
58 start = (page - 1) * limit
59 end = start + limit
60 paginated_users = users[start:end]
61
62 return jsonify({
63 'users': paginated_users,
64 'page': page,
65 'limit': limit,
66 'total': len(users)
67 })
68
69@app.route('/api/users', methods=['POST'])
70@require_auth
71def create_user():
72 """Create a new user."""
73 try:
74 data = request.get_json()
75
76 # Validate input
77 if not data or 'name' not in data or 'email' not in data:
78 return jsonify({'error': 'Name and email are required'}), 400
79
80 # Create user (in real app, save to database)
81 user = {
82 'id': 4, # In real app, generate unique ID
83 'name': data['name'],
84 'email': data['email']
85 }
86
87 return jsonify(user), 201
88
89except Exception as e:
90 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
91
92# Webhook endpoint with signature verification
93@app.route('/webhook/stripe', methods=['POST'])
94def stripe_webhook():
95 """Handle Stripe webhooks with signature verification."""
96 payload = request.get_data()
97 sig_header = request.headers.get('Stripe-Signature')
98 endpoint_secret = 'whsec_your_webhook_secret'
99
100 try:
101 # Verify webhook signature
102 timestamp, signatures = {}, {}
103 for item in sig_header.split(','):
104 key, value = item.split('=', 1)
105 if key == 't':
106 timestamp = value
107 elif key == 'v1':
108 signatures = value
109
110 signed_payload = f"{timestamp}.{payload.decode('utf-8')}"
111 expected_sig = hmac.new(
112 endpoint_secret.encode(),
113 signed_payload.encode(),
114 hashlib.sha256
115 ).hexdigest()
116
117 if not hmac.compare_digest(expected_sig, signatures):
118 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid signature'}), 400
119
120 # Process webhook event
121 event = request.get_json()
122 event_type = event.get('type')
123
124 if event_type == 'payment_intent.succeeded':
125 # Handle successful payment
126 print(f"Payment succeeded: {event['data']['object']['id']}")
127 elif event_type == 'payment_intent.payment_failed':
128 # Handle failed payment
129 print(f"Payment failed: {event['data']['object']['id']}")
130
131 return jsonify({'status': 'processed'})
132
133except Exception as e:
134 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
135
136# File upload endpoint
137@app.route('/upload', methods=['POST'])
138def upload_file():
139 """Handle file uploads."""
140 if 'file' not in request.files:
141 return jsonify({'error': 'No file provided'}), 400
142
143 file = request.files['file']
144 if file.filename == '':
145 return jsonify({'error': 'No file selected'}), 400
146
147 # Validate file type and size
148 allowed_extensions = {'png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', 'pdf'}
149 if '.' not in file.filename or file.filename.rsplit('.', 1)[1].lower() not in allowed_extensions:
150 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid file type'}), 400
151
152 if len(file.read()) > 10 * 1024 * 1024: # 10MB limit
153 return jsonify({'error': 'File too large'}), 400
154
155 file.seek(0) # Reset file pointer
156
157 # In real app, save to Cloud Storage
158 return jsonify({
159 'filename': file.filename,
160 'size': len(file.read()),
161 'status': 'uploaded'
162 })
javascript
1const express = require('express');
2const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
3const crypto = require('crypto');
4const multer = require('multer');
5const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');
6
7const app = express();
8app.use(express.json());
9
10const SECRET_KEY = 'your-secret-key';
11const upload = multer({
12 limits: { fileSize: 10 * 1024 * 1024 }, // 10MB
13 fileFilter: (req, file, cb) => {
14 const allowedTypes = /jpeg|jpg|png|gif|pdf/;
15 const extname = allowedTypes.test(path.extname(file.originalname).toLowerCase());
16 const mimetype = allowedTypes.test(file.mimetype);
17
18 if (mimetype && extname) {
19 return cb(null, true);
20 } else {
21 cb('Error: Invalid file type!');
22 }
23 }
24});
25
26// Rate limiting
27const limiter = rateLimit({
28 windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
29 max: 100, // limit each IP to 100 requests per windowMs
30 message: 'Too many requests from this IP, please try again later.'
31});
32app.use(limiter);
33
34// Authentication middleware
35const authenticateToken = (req, res, next) => {
36 const authHeader = req.headers['authorization'];
37 const token = authHeader && authHeader.split(' ')[1];
38
39 if (!token) {
40 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Access token required' });
41 }
42
43 jwt.verify(token, SECRET_KEY, (err, user) => {
44 if (err) {
45 return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Invalid token' });
46 }
47 req.user = user;
48 next();
49 });
50};
51
52// CORS middleware
53app.use((req, res, next) => {
54 res.header('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*');
55 res.header('Access-Control-Allow-Methods', 'GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS');
56 res.header('Access-Control-Allow-Headers', 'Origin, X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Accept, Authorization');
57 next();
58});
59
60// RESTful API endpoints
61app.get('/api/users', (req, res) => {
62 const { page = 1, limit = 10, search = '' } = req.query;
63
64 // Mock data
65 let users = [
66 { id: 1, name: 'Alice', email: 'alice@example.com' },
67 { id: 2, name: 'Bob', email: 'bob@example.com' },
68 { id: 3, name: 'Charlie', email: 'charlie@example.com' }
69 ];
70
71 // Apply search filter
72 if (search) {
73 users = users.filter(user =>
74 user.name.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()) ||
75 user.email.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase())
76 );
77 }
78
79 // Apply pagination
80 const startIndex = (page - 1) * limit;
81 const endIndex = startIndex + limit;
82 const paginatedUsers = users.slice(startIndex, endIndex);
83
84 res.json({
85 users: paginatedUsers,
86 page: parseInt(page),
87 limit: parseInt(limit),
88 total: users.length
89 });
90});
91
92app.post('/api/users', authenticateToken, (req, res) => {
93 const { name, email } = req.body;
94
95 if (!name || !email) {
96 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Name and email are required' });
97 }
98
99 const newUser = {
100 id: Date.now(), // Simple ID generation
101 name,
102 email
103 };
104
105 res.status(201).json(newUser);
106});
107
108// Webhook with signature verification
109app.post('/webhook/stripe', express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }), (req, res) => {
110 const sig = req.headers['stripe-signature'];
111 const endpointSecret = 'whsec_your_webhook_secret';
112
113 let event;
114
115 try {
116 event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(req.body, sig, endpointSecret);
117 } catch (err) {
118 console.log(`Webhook signature verification failed.`, err.message);
119 return res.status(400).send(`Webhook Error: ${err.message}`);
120 }
121
122 // Handle the event
123 switch (event.type) {
124 case 'payment_intent.succeeded':
125 console.log(`PaymentIntent was successful! ID: ${event.data.object.id}`);
126 break;
127 case 'payment_method.attached':
128 console.log(`PaymentMethod was attached to a Customer!`);
129 break;
130 default:
131 console.log(`Unhandled event type ${event.type}`);
132 }
133
134 res.json({ received: true });
135});
136
137// File upload endpoint
138app.post('/upload', upload.single('file'), (req, res) => {
139 if (!req.file) {
140 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'No file uploaded' });
141 }
142
res.json({
143 filename: req.file.originalname,
144 size: req.file.size,
145 mimetype: req.file.mimetype,
146 status: 'uploaded'
147 });
148});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "crypto/hmac"
67 "crypto/sha256"
8 "encoding/hex"
9 "encoding/json"
10 "fmt"
11 "io"
12 "log"
13 "net/http"
14 "strconv"
15 "strings"
16 "time"
17
18 "github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v4"
19 "github.com/gorilla/mux"
20 "golang.org/x/time/rate"
21)
22
23var (
24 jwtSecret = []byte("your-secret-key")
25 limiter = rate.NewLimiter(1, 100) // 1 request per second, burst of 100
26)
27
28// User represents a user in the system
29type User struct {
30 ID int `json:"id"`
31 Name string `json:"name"`
32 Email string `json:"email"`
33}
34
35// Claims represents JWT claims
36type Claims struct {
37 UserID int `json:"user_id"`
38 jwt.RegisteredClaims
39}
40
41// Rate limiting middleware
42func rateLimit(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
43 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
44 if !limiter.Allow() {
45 http.Error(w, "Rate limit exceeded", http.StatusTooManyRequests)
46 return
47 }
48 next(w, r)
49 }
50}
51
52// Authentication middleware
53func authenticateToken(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
54 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
55 authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
56 if authHeader == "" {
57 http.Error(w, "Authorization header required", http.StatusUnauthorized)
58 return
59 }
60
61 tokenString := strings.TrimPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ")
62 if tokenString == authHeader {
63 http.Error(w, "Invalid token format", http.StatusUnauthorized)
64 return
65 }
66
67 claims := &Claims{}
68 token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, claims, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {
69 return jwtSecret, nil
70 })
71
72 if err != nil || !token.Valid {
73 http.Error(w, "Invalid token", http.StatusUnauthorized)
74 return
75 }
76
77 ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), "user_id", claims.UserID)
78 next(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
79 }
80}
81
82// CORS middleware
83func corsMiddleware(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
84 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
85 w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
86 w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS")
87 w.Header().Set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", "Content-Type, Authorization")
88 if r.Method == "OPTIONS" {
89 w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
90 return
91 }
92 next(w, r)
93 }
94}
95
96// GetUsers handles GET /api/users
97func GetUsers(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
98 query := r.URL.Query()
99 page, _ := strconv.Atoi(query.Get("page"))
100 if page == 0 {
101 page = 1
102 }
103 limit, _ := strconv.Atoi(query.Get("limit"))
104 if limit == 0 {
105 limit = 10
106 }
107 search := query.Get("search")
108
109 // Mock data
110 users := []User{
111 {ID: 1, Name: "Alice", Email: "alice@example.com"},
112 {ID: 2, Name: "Bob", Email: "bob@example.com"},
113 {ID: 3, Name: "Charlie", Email: "charlie@example.com"},
114 }
115
116 // Apply search filter
117 if search != "" {
118 var filtered []User
119 for _, user := range users {
120 if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(user.Name), strings.ToLower(search)) ||
121 strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(user.Email), strings.ToLower(search)) {
122 filtered = append(filtered, user)
123 }
124 }
125 users = filtered
126 }
127
128 // Apply pagination
129 startIndex := (page - 1) * limit
130 endIndex := startIndex + limit
131 if startIndex > len(users) {
132 startIndex = len(users)
133 }
134 if endIndex > len(users) {
135 endIndex = len(users)
136 }
137 paginatedUsers := users[startIndex:endIndex]
138
139 response := map[string]interface{}{
140 "users": paginatedUsers,
141 "page": page,
142 "limit": limit,
143 "total": len(users),
144 }
145
146 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
147 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
148}
149
150// CreateUser handles POST /api/users
151func CreateUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
152 var user User
153 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&user); err != nil {
154 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
155 return
156 }
157
158 if user.Name == "" || user.Email == "" {
159 http.Error(w, "Name and email are required", http.StatusBadRequest)
160 return
161 }
162
163 user.ID = int(time.Now().Unix()) // Simple ID generation
164
165 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
166 w.WriteHeader(http.StatusCreated)
167 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(user)
168}
169
170// StripeWebhook handles Stripe webhook events
171func StripeWebhook(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
172 body, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
173 if err != nil {
174 http.Error(w, "Error reading request body", http.StatusBadRequest)
175 return
176 }
177
178 sig := r.Header.Get("Stripe-Signature")
179 endpointSecret := "whsec_your_webhook_secret"
180
181 // Verify signature
182 parts := strings.Split(sig, ",")
183 timestamp := ""
184 signature := ""
185 for _, part := range parts {
186 kv := strings.SplitN(part, "=", 2)
187 if len(kv) == 2 {
188 if kv[0] == "t" {
189 timestamp = kv[1]
190 } else if kv[0] == "v1" {
191 signature = kv[1]
192 }
193 }
194 }
195
196 signedPayload := timestamp + "." + string(body)
197 h := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(endpointSecret))
198 h.Write([]byte(signedPayload))
199 expectedSignature := hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))
200
201 if !hmac.Equal([]byte(expectedSignature), []byte(signature)) {
202 http.Error(w, "Invalid signature", http.StatusBadRequest)
203 return
204 }
205
206 var event map[string]interface{}
207 json.Unmarshal(body, &event)
208
209 eventType := event["type"].(string)
210 switch eventType {
211 case "payment_intent.succeeded":
212 log.Printf("PaymentIntent was successful! ID: %v", event["data"].(map[string]interface{})["object"].(map[string]interface{})["id"])
213 case "payment_method.attached":
214 log.Println("PaymentMethod was attached to a Customer!")
215 default:
216 log.Printf("Unhandled event type %s", eventType)
217 }
218
219 w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
220 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]bool{"received": true})
221}
🌐 HTTP Function Best Practices
  • Request Validation: Always validate input data and handle malformed requests
  • Authentication: Implement proper authentication for sensitive endpoints
  • CORS: Configure CORS headers for web applications
  • Rate Limiting: Protect against abuse with rate limiting
  • Error Handling: Return appropriate HTTP status codes and error messages
  • Security: Validate webhooks with signature verification
📦

Dependencies & Packages

Intermediate

Cloud Functions support external dependencies through package managers. Understanding dependency management is crucial for function reliability and performance.

Dependency Management Examples
python
1# requirements.txt
2flask==2.3.3
3requests==2.31.0
4google-cloud-storage==2.10.0
5google-cloud-pubsub==2.18.4
6google-cloud-firestore==2.11.1
7pandas==2.0.3
8numpy==1.24.3
9pillow==10.0.0
10opencv-python-headless==4.8.0.76
11
12# main.py
13from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
14import requests
15from google.cloud import storage
16import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
17from PIL import Image
18import cv2
19import io
20from google.cloud import functions_framework
21
22app = Flask(__name__)
23storage_client = storage.Client()
24
25# Data processing function with pandas/numpy
26@functions_framework.http
27def process_data(request):
28 """Process CSV data using pandas and numpy."""
29 try:
30 # Get CSV data from request
31 csv_data = request.get_data().decode('utf-8')
32
33 # Process with pandas
34 df = pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))
35
36 # Perform calculations with numpy
37 numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns
38 stats = {
39 'mean': df[numeric_cols].mean().to_dict(),
40 'std': df[numeric_cols].std().to_dict(),
41 'count': len(df)
42 }
43
44 return jsonify({'status': 'processed', 'stats': stats})
45
46 except Exception as e:
47 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
48
49# Image processing function
50@functions_framework.http
51def process_image(request):
52 """Process image using OpenCV and PIL."""
53 try:
54 # Get image from Cloud Storage
55 bucket_name = request.args.get('bucket')
56 file_name = request.args.get('file')
57
58 bucket = storage_client.bucket(bucket_name)
59 blob = bucket.blob(file_name)
60
61 # Download image
62 image_data = blob.download_as_bytes()
63 image = Image.open(io.BytesIO(image_data))
64
65 # Convert to OpenCV format
66 opencv_image = cv2.cvtColor(np.array(image), cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)
67
68 # Process image (e.g., resize)
69 height, width = opencv_image.shape[:2]
70 resized = cv2.resize(opencv_image, (width//2, height//2))
71
72 result = {
73 'original_size': f"{width}x{height}",
74 'processed_size': f"{width//2}x{height//2}",
75 'format': image.format
76 }
77
return jsonify(result)
78
79 except Exception as e:
80 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 400
javascript
1// package.json
2{
3 "name": "cloud-functions-example",
4 "version": "1.0.0",
5 "dependencies": {
6 "express": "^4.18.2",
7 "axios": "^1.4.0",
8 "@google-cloud/storage": "^6.12.0",
9 "@google-cloud/pubsub": "^3.7.0",
10 "@google-cloud/firestore": "^6.7.0",
11 "sharp": "^0.32.4",
12 "csv-parser": "^3.0.0",
13 "moment": "^2.29.4",
14 "lodash": "^4.17.21",
15 "uuid": "^9.0.0"
16 }
17}
18
19// index.js
20const express = require('express');
21const axios = require('axios');
22const {Storage} = require('@google-cloud/storage');
23const sharp = require('sharp');
24const csv = require('csv-parser');
25const moment = require('moment');
26const _ = require('lodash');
27const {v4: uuidv4} = require('uuid');
28
29const app = express();
30app.use(express.json());
31const storage = new Storage();
32
33// Data processing with CSV parser and lodash
34exports.processCSV = async (req, res) => {
35 try {
36 const csvData = req.body.csv;
37 const results = [];
38
39 // Parse CSV data
40 const stream = require('stream');
41 const readableStream = new stream.Readable();
42 readableStream.push(csvData);
43 readableStream.push(null);
44
45 readableStream
46 .pipe(csv())
47 .on('data', (data) => results.push(data))
48 .on('end', () => {
49 // Process with lodash
50 const processed = _.chain(results)
51 .groupBy('category')
52 .mapValues((group) => ({
53 count: group.length,
54 total: _.sumBy(group, (item) => parseFloat(item.value) || 0)
55 }))
56 .value();
57
58 res.json({ processed, totalRecords: results.length });
59 });
60 } catch (error) {
61 res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
62 }
63};
64
65// Image processing with Sharp
66exports.processImage = async (req, res) => {
67 try {
68 const bucketName = req.query.bucket;
69 const fileName = req.query.file;
70
const bucket = storage.bucket(bucketName);
71 const file = bucket.file(fileName);
72
73 // Download image
74 const [buffer] = await file.download();
75
76 // Process with Sharp
77 const processedBuffer = await sharp(buffer)
78 .resize(800, 600, { fit: 'inside' })
79 .jpeg({ quality: 80 })
80 .toBuffer();
81
82 // Save processed image
83 const processedFileName = `processed/${uuidv4()}.jpg`;
84 const processedFile = bucket.file(processedFileName);
85 await processedFile.save(processedBuffer, {
86 metadata: { contentType: 'image/jpeg' }
87 });
88
89 res.json({
90 original: fileName,
91 processed: processedFileName,
92 size: processedBuffer.length
93 });
94 } catch (error) {
95 res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
96 }
97};
go
1module cloud-functions-example
2
3go 1.19
4
5require (
6 cloud.google.com/go/storage v1.30.1
7 cloud.google.com/go/pubsub v1.33.0
8 cloud.google.com/go/firestore v1.14.0
9 github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.0
10 github.com/google/uuid v1.3.0
11 gonum.org/v1/gonum v0.13.0
12 github.com/disintegration/imaging v1.6.2
13 github.com/360EntSecGroup-Skylar/excelize/v2 v2.7.1
14 github.com/joho/godotenv v1.4.0
15)
16
17package function
18
19import (
20 "context"
21 "encoding/json"
22 "fmt"
23 "io"
24 "log"
25 "net/http"
26 "strconv"
27 "strings"
28
29 "cloud.google.com/go/storage"
30 "github.com/google/uuid"
31 "gonum.org/v1/gonum/mat"
32 "github.com/disintegration/imaging"
33 "github.com/360EntSecGroup-Skylar/excelize/v2"
34 "github.com/joho/godotenv"
35)
36
37// ProcessData processes numerical data using Gonum
38func ProcessData(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
39 var data []float64
40 json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&data)
41
42 // Create matrix from data
43 matrix := mat.NewDense(len(data), 1, data)
44
45 // Calculate statistics
46 var sum float64
47 for _, val := range data {
48 sum += val
49 }
50 mean := sum / float64(len(data))
51
52 result := map[string]interface{}{
53 "count": len(data),
54 "mean": mean,
55 "sum": sum,
56 }
57
58 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
59 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)
60}
61
62// ProcessImage processes images using imaging library
63func ProcessImage(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
64 bucketName := r.URL.Query().Get("bucket")
65 fileName := r.URL.Query().Get("file")
66
ctx := context.Background()
67 client, err := storage.NewClient(ctx)
68 if err != nil {
69 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
70 return
71 }
72 defer client.Close()
73
74 bucket := client.Bucket(bucketName)
75 obj := bucket.Object(fileName)
76 reader, err := obj.NewReader(ctx)
77 if err != nil {
78 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
79 return
80 }
81 defer reader.Close()
82
83 // Decode image
84 src, err := imaging.Decode(reader)
85 if err != nil {
86 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
87 return
88 }
89
90 // Process image
91 dst := imaging.Resize(src, 800, 600, imaging.Lanczos)
92 dst = imaging.AdjustBrightness(dst, 10)
93
94 // Save processed image
95 processedFileName := fmt.Sprintf("processed/%s.jpg", uuid.New().String())
96 obj = bucket.Object(processedFileName)
97 writer := obj.NewWriter(ctx)
98 defer writer.Close()
99
100 err = imaging.Encode(writer, dst, imaging.JPEG)
101 if err != nil {
102 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
103 return
104 }
105
106 result := map[string]string{
107 "original": fileName,
108 "processed": processedFileName,
109 }
110
111 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
112 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)
113}
📦 Dependency Management Best Practices
  • Version Pinning: Use specific versions to ensure reproducible deployments
  • Minimal Dependencies: Only include necessary packages to reduce cold start times
  • Security Updates: Regularly update dependencies for security patches
  • Size Limits: Be aware of deployment package size limits (100MB compressed)
  • Native Libraries: Some packages require specific runtime configurations
🤔

When to Use Cloud Functions

Intermediate

Cloud Functions is Google's serverless compute service for event-driven functions. Understanding when to choose Cloud Functions versus other GCP compute services is crucial for optimal architecture design.

🚀 Cloud Functions Selection Decision Tree
Do you need event-driven processing?
Yes → Continue
No → Consider Cloud Run or GKE
Is your workload short-running (<15min)?
Yes → Continue
No → Consider Cloud Run or GKE
Do you need single-purpose functions?
Yes → Cloud Functions
No → Consider Cloud Run
⚖️ Cloud Functions vs Other GCP Compute Services
Aspect Cloud Functions Cloud Run GKE Compute Engine
Use Case Event-driven functions, microservices, webhooks Containerized apps, APIs, web services Complex applications, stateful services Full control, custom infrastructure
Scaling Automatic, per-function Automatic, container-based Manual/Auto, pod-based Manual scaling
Cold Starts Yes (can be minimized) Minimal (containers stay warm) No (persistent pods) No (persistent VMs)
Execution Time Max 15 minutes Up to 60 minutes Unlimited Unlimited
State Stateless only Stateless preferred Stateful possible Stateful possible
Cost Model Per invocation + compute time Per request + compute time Per node + compute time Per VM instance
Best For Event processing, automation, light APIs Web apps, APIs, microservices Complex apps, ML workloads Legacy apps, custom requirements
🔄 Migration Scenarios to Cloud Functions

📨 Migrating Webhooks from Traditional Servers

Before: Monolithic server handling multiple webhook endpoints

After: Individual Cloud Functions for each webhook type

Webhook Function Example
python
1import json
2from datetime import datetime
3from google.cloud import firestore
4from functions_framework import http
5
6db = firestore.Client()
7
8@http
9def stripe_webhook(request):
10 """Process Stripe webhook events."""
11 try:
12 # Verify webhook signature
13 signature = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')
14 if not verify_stripe_signature(request.data, signature):
15 return ('Unauthorized', 401)
16
17 event_data = json.loads(request.data)
18 event_type = event_data['type']
19
20 # Route to appropriate handler
21 if event_type == 'payment_intent.succeeded':
22 result = handle_payment_success(event_data)
23 elif event_type == 'customer.subscription.created':
24 result = handle_subscription_created(event_data)
25 else:
26 result = f"Unhandled event type: {event_type}"
27
28 return (json.dumps({'status': 'processed', 'result': result}), 200)
29
30 except Exception as e:
31 return (json.dumps({'error': str(e)}), 500)
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const crypto = require('crypto');
3const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
4const firestore = new Firestore();
5
6functions.http('stripeWebhook', async (req, res) => {
7 try {
8 // Verify Stripe webhook signature
9 const signature = req.headers['stripe-signature'];
10 if (!verifyStripeSignature(req.rawBody, signature)) {
11 return res.status(401).json({error: 'Unauthorized'});
12 }
13
14 const event = JSON.parse(req.body);
15 const eventType = event.type;
16
17 // Route to appropriate handler
18 let result;
19 switch (eventType) {
20 case 'payment_intent.succeeded':
21 result = await handlePaymentSuccess(event);
22 break;
23 case 'customer.subscription.created':
24 result = await handleSubscriptionCreated(event);
25 break;
26 default:
27 result = `Unhandled event type: ${eventType}`;
28 }
29
30 res.json({status: 'processed', result});
31 } catch (error) {
32 console.error('Webhook processing error:', error);
33 res.status(500).json({error: error.message});
34 }
35});

⏰ Migrating Cron Jobs from Virtual Machines

Before: Cron jobs running on dedicated VMs

After: Scheduled Cloud Functions with Cloud Scheduler

Scheduled Function Example
python
1import json
2from datetime import datetime
3from google.cloud import pubsub_v1
4from functions_framework import cloud_event
5
6publisher = pubsub_v1.PublisherClient()
7
8@cloud_event
9def daily_report(cloudevent):
10 """Generate daily usage reports."""
11 try:
12 # Generate report data
13 report_data = generate_usage_report()
14
15 # Send email notification
16 send_report_email(report_data)
17
18 # Archive report
19 archive_report(report_data)
20
21 return f"Daily report generated for {datetime.now().date()}"
22
23 except Exception as e:
24 return f"Error generating daily report: {str(e)}"
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "log"
7 "net/http"
8 "time"
9)
10
11func DailyReport(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
12 ctx := context.Background()
13
14 // Generate report data
15 reportData, err := generateUsageReport(ctx)
16 if err != nil {
17 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
18 return
19 }
20
21 // Send email notification
22 if err := sendReportEmail(ctx, reportData); err != nil {
23 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
24 return
25 }
26
27 // Archive report
28 if err := archiveReport(ctx, reportData); err != nil {
29 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
30 return
31 }
32
33 response := map[string]string{
34 "status": "completed",
35 "report_date": time.Now().Format("2006-01-02"),
36 }
37
38 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
39 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
40}

🔌 Migrating Lightweight APIs from App Engine

Before: Multiple endpoints in a monolithic App Engine service

After: Individual Cloud Functions for each API endpoint

API Function Example
python
1import json
2from flask import jsonify, request
3from google.cloud import firestore
4from functions_framework import http
5
6db = firestore.Client()
7
8@http
9def get_user_profile(request):
10 """Get user profile by ID."""
11 try:
12 user_id = request.args.get('user_id')
13 if not user_id:
14 return jsonify({'error': 'user_id parameter required'}), 400
15
16 # Get user document
17 user_doc = db.collection('users').document(user_id).get()
18 if not user_doc.exists:
19 return jsonify({'error': 'User not found'}), 404
20
21 user_data = user_doc.to_dict()
22 # Remove sensitive fields
23 user_data.pop('password_hash', None)
24 user_data.pop('internal_notes', None)
25
26 return jsonify(user_data), 200
27
28 except Exception as e:
29 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
3const firestore = new Firestore();
4
5functions.http('getUserProfile', async (req, res) => {
6 try {
7 const {userId} = req.query;
8
9 if (!userId) {
10 return res.status(400).json({
11 error: 'userId query parameter is required'
12 });
13 }
14
15 // Get user document
16 const userDoc = await firestore.collection('users').doc(userId).get();
17
18 if (!userDoc.exists) {
19 return res.status(404).json({error: 'User not found'});
20 }
21
22 const userData = userDoc.data();
23
24 // Remove sensitive fields
25 delete userData.passwordHash;
26 delete userData.internalNotes;
27
28 res.json(userData);
29 } catch (error) {
30 console.error('Error getting user profile:', error);
31 res.status(500).json({error: error.message});
32 }
33});
⚠️ Cloud Functions Usage Warnings
  • Execution Limits: Functions timeout after 15 minutes maximum
  • Cold Starts: First invocation may experience latency (use min instances for critical workloads)
  • Stateless Only: Cannot maintain state between invocations
  • Resource Limits: Memory capped at 8GB, CPU scales with memory
  • Cost Scaling: Pay per invocation and compute time - monitor usage carefully
  • Debugging: Limited debugging capabilities compared to full applications
  • Vendor Lock-in: GCP-specific service with limited portability
✅ Cloud Functions Best Practices
  • Single Responsibility: Each function should do one thing well
  • Idempotent Operations: Design functions to handle duplicate executions safely
  • Error Handling: Implement comprehensive error handling and logging
  • Environment Variables: Use for configuration, not secrets (use Secret Manager)
  • Testing: Test functions locally and in staging before production deployment
  • Monitoring: Set up Cloud Monitoring and Logging for observability
  • Cost Optimization: Use appropriate memory allocation and monitor usage patterns
🎯

Triggers & Event Sources

Intermediate

Cloud Functions can be triggered by various events from Google Cloud services. Each trigger type provides different event data and invocation patterns.

⚡ Function Execution Flow: Trigger → Function → Runtime → Response
🎯 Trigger
Event
⚡ Function
Code
🏃 Runtime
Environment
📤 Response
Result
Serverless Execution Pipeline
gcloud: Deploy Functions with Different Triggers
bash
1# Deploy HTTP function
2gcloud functions deploy hello-http \
3 --runtime=nodejs16 \
4 --trigger-http \
5 --allow-unauthenticated \
6 --region=us-central1
7
8# Deploy Cloud Storage trigger
9gcloud functions deploy hello-gcs \
10 --runtime=python39 \
11 --trigger-resource=my-bucket \
12 --trigger-event=google.storage.object.finalize \
13 --region=us-central1
14
15# Deploy Pub/Sub trigger
16gcloud functions deploy hello-pubsub \
17 --runtime=go116 \
18 --trigger-topic=my-topic \
19 --region=us-central1
20
21# Deploy Firestore trigger
22gcloud functions deploy hello-firestore \
23 --runtime=nodejs16 \
24 --trigger-event=providers/cloud.firestore/eventTypes/document.write \
25 --trigger-resource="projects/my-project/databases/(default)/documents/my-collection/{documentId}" \
26 --region=us-central1
27
28# Deploy Cloud Scheduler trigger
29gcloud functions deploy scheduled-function \
30 --runtime=python39 \
31 --trigger-topic=my-scheduled-topic \
32 --region=us-central1
33
34# Create scheduler job
35gcloud scheduler jobs create pubsub scheduled-job \
36 --schedule="0 */2 * * *" \
37 --topic=my-scheduled-topic \
38 --message-body="Scheduled execution" \
39 --region=us-central1
hcl
1resource "google_cloudfunctions_function" "http_function" {
2 name = "hello-http"
3 runtime = "nodejs16"
4 region = "us-central1"
5
6 source_archive_bucket = google_storage_bucket.function_bucket.name
7 source_archive_object = google_storage_bucket_object.function_zip.name
8
9 trigger_http = true
10
11 entry_point = "helloHttp"
12}
13
14# Cloud Storage trigger
15resource "google_cloudfunctions_function" "storage_function" {
16 name = "hello-gcs"
17 runtime = "python39"
18 region = "us-central1"
19
20 source_archive_bucket = google_storage_bucket.function_bucket.name
21 source_archive_object = google_storage_bucket_object.function_zip.name
22
23 event_trigger {
24 event_type = "google.storage.object.finalize"
25 resource = google_storage_bucket.my_bucket.name
26 }
27
28 entry_point = "hello_gcs"
29}
🎯 Trigger Types
  • HTTP: Direct web requests and API calls
  • Cloud Storage: File upload, deletion, and metadata changes
  • Pub/Sub: Asynchronous messaging and event streaming
  • Firestore: Database document changes and updates
  • Firebase: Realtime Database and authentication events
  • Cloud Scheduler: Time-based triggers and cron jobs
📦

Deployment & Configuration

Intermediate

Deploying Cloud Functions involves configuring runtime, memory, timeout, and environment variables. Understanding these settings is crucial for optimal performance and cost.

Advanced Deployment Options
bash
1# Deploy with custom memory and timeout
2gcloud functions deploy my-function \
3 --runtime=nodejs16 \
4 --memory=2048MB \
5 --timeout=300s \
6 --max-instances=100 \
7 --trigger-http \
8 --region=us-central1
9
10# Deploy with environment variables
11gcloud functions deploy env-function \
12 --runtime=python39 \
13 --set-env-vars="API_KEY=secret,DEBUG=true" \
14 --trigger-http \
15 --region=us-central1
16
17# Deploy with VPC connector (private networking)
18gcloud functions deploy vpc-function \
19 --runtime=nodejs16 \
20 --vpc-connector=my-connector \
21 --egress-settings=all \
22 --trigger-http \
23 --region=us-central1
24
25# Deploy with custom service account
26gcloud functions deploy service-account-function \
27 --runtime=go116 \
28 --service-account=my-service@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com \
29 --trigger-topic=my-topic \
30 --region=us-central1
31
32# Deploy with minimum instances (no cold starts)
33gcloud functions deploy warm-function \
34 --runtime=nodejs16 \
35 --min-instances=1 \
36 --trigger-http \
37 --region=us-central1
38
39# Update function configuration
40gcloud functions deploy my-function \
41 --runtime=nodejs16 \
42 --memory=4096MB \
43 --update-labels="version=v2,team=backend" \
44 --region=us-central1
hcl
1resource "google_cloudfunctions_function" "advanced_function" {
2 name = "my-advanced-function"
3 runtime = "nodejs16"
4 region = "us-central1"
5 memory_size = 2048
6 timeout = 300
7 max_instances = 100
8
9 source_archive_bucket = google_storage_bucket.function_bucket.name
10 source_archive_object = google_storage_bucket_object.function_zip.name
11
12 trigger_http = true
13
14 environment_variables = {
15 API_KEY = "secret"
16 DEBUG = "true"
17 }
18
19 vpc_connector = google_vpc_access_connector.my_connector.name
20 vpc_connector_egress_settings = "ALL_TRAFFIC"
21
22 service_account_email = google_service_account.function_sa.email
23
24 entry_point = "myFunction"
25}
📦 Deployment Best Practices
  • Memory Allocation: Start with 256MB, monitor and adjust based on usage
  • Timeout Settings: Set appropriate timeouts to prevent runaway functions
  • Min Instances: Use for latency-sensitive applications to avoid cold starts
  • Environment Variables: Store configuration separately from code
  • VPC Connectors: Access private resources securely
🌍

Runtime Environment

Intermediate

Cloud Functions runtime environments provide isolated execution contexts with specific language versions, system libraries, and execution limits. Understanding runtime configuration is essential for optimal function performance.

Runtime Configuration Examples
python
1import os
2import sys
3import platform
4from google.cloud import functions_framework
5
6# Environment inspection function
7@functions_framework.http
8def environment_info(request):
9 """Return runtime environment information."""
10 env_info = {
11 'python_version': sys.version,
12 'platform': platform.platform(),
13 'architecture': platform.machine(),
14 'memory_limit': os.environ.get('FUNCTION_MEMORY_MB', 'Unknown'),
15 'timeout': os.environ.get('FUNCTION_TIMEOUT_SEC', 'Unknown'),
16 'region': os.environ.get('FUNCTION_REGION', 'Unknown'),
17 'project': os.environ.get('GCP_PROJECT', 'Unknown'),
18 'environment_variables': {
19 key: value for key, value in os.environ.items()
20 if key.startswith('CUSTOM_') or key in ['NODE_ENV', 'DEBUG']
21 }
22 }
23
24 return jsonify(env_info)
25
26# Temporary file management
27@functions_framework.http
28def temp_file_demo(request):
29 """Demonstrate temporary file usage in functions."""
30 import tempfile
31 import json
32
33 # Use /tmp directory for temporary files
34 with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.json', delete=False) as f:
35 temp_data = {'timestamp': str(datetime.now()), 'data': 'example'}
36 json.dump(temp_data, f)
37 temp_file_path = f.name
38
39 # Read back the temporary file
40 with open(temp_file_path, 'r') as f:
41 loaded_data = json.load(f)
42
43 # Clean up
44 os.unlink(temp_file_path)
45
46 return jsonify({'temp_file_demo': loaded_data})
javascript
1const os = require('os');
2const fs = require('fs');
3const path = require('path');
4
5// Environment inspection
6exports.environmentInfo = (req, res) => {
7 const envInfo = {
8 nodeVersion: process.version,
9 platform: process.platform,
10 architecture: process.arch,
11 memoryLimit: process.env.FUNCTION_MEMORY_MB || 'Unknown',
12 timeout: process.env.FUNCTION_TIMEOUT_SEC || 'Unknown',
13 region: process.env.FUNCTION_REGION || 'Unknown',
14 project: process.env.GCP_PROJECT || 'Unknown',
15 environmentVariables: Object.keys(process.env)
16 .filter(key => key.startsWith('CUSTOM_') || ['NODE_ENV', 'DEBUG'].includes(key))
17 .reduce((obj, key) => {
18 obj[key] = process.env[key];
19 return obj;
20 }, {}),
21 availableMemory: os.totalmem(),
22 freeMemory: os.freemem(),
23 cpuCount: os.cpus().length
24 };
25
26 res.json(envInfo);
27};
28
29// Temporary file management
30exports.tempFileDemo = (req, res) => {
31 const tempDir = os.tmpdir();
32 const tempFilePath = path.join(tempDir, `temp-${Date.now()}.json`);
33
34 const tempData = {
35 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
36 data: 'example',
37 memoryUsage: process.memoryUsage()
38 };
39
40 // Write to temporary file
41 fs.writeFileSync(tempFilePath, JSON.stringify(tempData, null, 2));
42
43 // Read back the file
44 const loadedData = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(tempFilePath, 'utf8'));
45
46 // Clean up
47 fs.unlinkSync(tempFilePath);
48
49 res.json({ tempFileDemo: loadedData });
50};
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
67 "io/ioutil"
8 "log"
9 "os"
10 "runtime"
11 "time"
12)
13
14// EnvironmentInfo returns runtime environment details
15func EnvironmentInfo(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
16 envInfo := map[string]interface{}{
17 "go_version": runtime.Version(),
18 "os": runtime.GOOS,
19 "architecture": runtime.GOARCH,
20 "cpu_count": runtime.NumCPU(),
21 "memory_limit": os.Getenv("FUNCTION_MEMORY_MB"),
22 "timeout": os.Getenv("FUNCTION_TIMEOUT_SEC"),
23 "region": os.Getenv("FUNCTION_REGION"),
24 "project": os.Getenv("GCP_PROJECT"),
25 }
26
27 // Get custom environment variables
28 customEnv := make(map[string]string)
29 for _, env := range os.Environ() {
30 if strings.HasPrefix(env, "CUSTOM_") {
31 parts := strings.SplitN(env, "=", 2)
32 if len(parts) == 2 {
33 customEnv[parts[0]] = parts[1]
34 }
35 }
36 }
37 envInfo["custom_env"] = customEnv
38
39 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
40 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(envInfo)
41}
42
43// TempFileDemo demonstrates temporary file usage
44func TempFileDemo(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
45 tempData := map[string]interface{}{
46 "timestamp": time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
47 "data": "example",
48 "goroutines": runtime.NumGoroutine(),
49 }
50
51 // Create temporary file
52 tempFile, err := ioutil.TempFile("", "temp-*.json")
53 if err != nil {
54 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
55 return
56 }
57 defer os.Remove(tempFile.Name()) // Clean up
58
59 // Write data to temp file
60 jsonData, _ := json.MarshalIndent(tempData, "", " ")
61 tempFile.Write(jsonData)
62 tempFile.Close()
63
64 // Read back the data
65 fileData, err := ioutil.ReadFile(tempFile.Name())
66 if err != nil {
67 http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
68 return
69 }
70
71 var loadedData map[string]interface{}
72 json.Unmarshal(fileData, &loadedData)
73
74 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
75 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{"tempFileDemo": loadedData})
76}
🌍 Runtime Environment Facts
  • Isolation: Each function runs in its own secure sandbox environment
  • Ephemeral: Runtime environments are created on-demand and destroyed after execution
  • Limits: Memory, CPU, and execution time are strictly enforced per function
  • Networking: Outbound connections are allowed, inbound only through triggers
  • Storage: /tmp directory provides temporary storage (limited space)
🌍

Environment Variables

Basic

Environment variables provide configuration data to your Cloud Functions without hardcoding values in your code. They enable secure storage of API keys, database credentials, and other sensitive information.

Environment Variable Configuration
python
1import os
2import functions_framework
3from flask import jsonify
4
5@functions_framework.http
6def environment_demo(request):
7 """Demonstrate environment variable usage."""
8
9 # Access environment variables
10 api_key = os.environ.get('API_KEY')
11 database_url = os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL')
12 debug_mode = os.environ.get('DEBUG', 'false').lower() == 'true'
13 max_retries = int(os.environ.get('MAX_RETRIES', '3'))
14
15 # Built-in Cloud Functions environment variables
16 function_name = os.environ.get('FUNCTION_NAME')
17 function_region = os.environ.get('FUNCTION_REGION')
18 function_memory = os.environ.get('FUNCTION_MEMORY_MB')
19 function_timeout = os.environ.get('FUNCTION_TIMEOUT_SEC')
20
21 # Validate required environment variables
22 if not api_key:
23 return jsonify({'error': 'API_KEY environment variable is required'}), 500
24
25 return jsonify({
26 'api_key_configured': bool(api_key),
27 'database_url_configured': bool(database_url),
28 'debug_mode': debug_mode,
29 'max_retries': max_retries,
30 'function_name': function_name,
31 'function_region': function_region,
32 'function_memory': function_memory,
33 'function_timeout': function_timeout
34 })
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2
3// Environment variable demonstration
4functions.http('environmentDemo', (req, res) => {
5 try {
6 // Access environment variables
7 const apiKey = process.env.API_KEY;
8 const databaseUrl = process.env.DATABASE_URL;
9 const debugMode = process.env.DEBUG === 'true';
10 const maxRetries = parseInt(process.env.MAX_RETRIES || '3');
11
12 // Built-in Cloud Functions environment variables
13 const functionName = process.env.FUNCTION_NAME;
14 const functionRegion = process.env.FUNCTION_REGION;
15 const functionMemory = process.env.FUNCTION_MEMORY_MB;
16 const functionTimeout = process.env.FUNCTION_TIMEOUT_SEC;
17
18 // Validate required environment variables
19 if (!apiKey) {
20 return res.status(500).json({
21 error: 'API_KEY environment variable is required'
22 });
23 }
24
25 res.json({
26 apiKeyConfigured: !!apiKey,
27 databaseUrlConfigured: !!databaseUrl,
28 debugMode,
29 maxRetries,
30 functionName,
31 functionRegion,
32 functionMemory,
33 functionTimeout
34 });
35 } catch (error) {
36 console.error('Environment demo error:', error);
37 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
38 }
39});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "encoding/json"
5 "net/http"
6 "os"
7 "strconv"
8)
9
10type EnvResponse struct {
11 APIKeyConfigured bool `json:"apiKeyConfigured"`
12 DatabaseURLConfigured bool `json:"databaseUrlConfigured"`
13 DebugMode bool `json:"debugMode"`
14 MaxRetries int `json:"maxRetries"`
15 FunctionName string `json:"functionName"`
16 FunctionRegion string `json:"functionRegion"`
17 FunctionMemory string `json:"functionMemory"`
18 FunctionTimeout string `json:"functionTimeout"`
19}
20
21func EnvironmentDemo(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
22 // Access environment variables
23 apiKey := os.Getenv("API_KEY")
24 databaseURL := os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL")
25 debugMode := os.Getenv("DEBUG") == "true"
26 maxRetriesStr := os.Getenv("MAX_RETRIES")
27 maxRetries := 3
28 if maxRetriesStr != "" {
29 if parsed, err := strconv.Atoi(maxRetriesStr); err == nil {
30 maxRetries = parsed
31 }
32 }
33
34 // Built-in Cloud Functions environment variables
35 functionName := os.Getenv("FUNCTION_NAME")
36 functionRegion := os.Getenv("FUNCTION_REGION")
37 functionMemory := os.Getenv("FUNCTION_MEMORY_MB")
38 functionTimeout := os.Getenv("FUNCTION_TIMEOUT_SEC")
39
40 // Validate required environment variables
41 if apiKey == "" {
42 http.Error(w, `{"error": "API_KEY environment variable is required"}`, http.StatusInternalServerError)
43 return
44 }
45
46 response := EnvResponse{
47 APIKeyConfigured: apiKey != "",
48 DatabaseURLConfigured: databaseURL != "",
49 DebugMode: debugMode,
50 MaxRetries: maxRetries,
51 FunctionName: functionName,
52 FunctionRegion: functionRegion,
53 FunctionMemory: functionMemory,
54 FunctionTimeout: functionTimeout,
55 }
56
57 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
58 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
59}
🔧 Environment Variable Best Practices
  • Security: Never hardcode sensitive data; use environment variables for API keys and credentials
  • Validation: Always validate required environment variables at startup
  • Defaults: Provide sensible defaults for optional configuration
  • Documentation: Document all required and optional environment variables
  • Scopes: Use different variable names for different environments (dev/staging/prod)
  • Secrets: Use Secret Manager for highly sensitive data instead of plain environment variables
📡

Cloud Events

Advanced

Cloud Events is a specification for describing event data in a common way. Cloud Functions supports Cloud Events for event-driven processing, enabling standardized event handling across different platforms and services.

📡 Cloud Events Flow: Event Source → Cloud Functions → Event Consumers
📤 Event Source
Generate Event
⚡ Cloud Function
Process Event
📨 Consumers
Receive Results
Standardized Event-Driven Architecture
Cloud Events Function
python
1import json
2from datetime import datetime
3from google.events.cloud.firestore import DocumentEventData
4from google.events.cloud.pubsub import MessagePublishedData
5from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent
6from google.cloud import firestore
7from functions_framework import cloud_event
8
9 firestore_client = firestore.Client()
10
11def process_firestore_event(cloudevent):
12 """Process Firestore document changes using Cloud Events."""
13 try:
14 # Parse Cloud Event
15 event = CloudEvent(cloudevent)
16 firestore_data = event.data
17
18 # Extract Firestore event data
19 document_name = firestore_data['value']['name']
20 document_fields = firestore_data['value']['fields']
21 event_type = event['type']
22
23 # Extract document ID from path
24 document_id = document_name.split('/')[-1]
25
26 # Convert Firestore fields to Python dict
27 user_data = {}
28 for field_name, field_value in document_fields.items():
29 if 'stringValue' in field_value:
30 user_data[field_name] = field_value['stringValue']
31 elif 'integerValue' in field_value:
32 user_data[field_name] = int(field_value['integerValue'])
33 elif 'booleanValue' in field_value:
34 user_data[field_name] = field_value['booleanValue']
35
36 # Process based on event type
37 if event_type == 'google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.created':
38 result = handle_user_created(document_id, user_data)
39 elif event_type == 'google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.updated':
40 result = handle_user_updated(document_id, user_data)
41 elif event_type == 'google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.deleted':
42 result = handle_user_deleted(document_id)
43 else:
44 result = f"Unhandled event type: {event_type}"
45
46 return f"Processed {event_type} for user {document_id}: {result}"
47
48except Exception as e:
49 return f"Error processing Firestore event: {str(e)}"
50
51def process_pubsub_event(cloudevent):
52 """Process Pub/Sub messages using Cloud Events."""
53 try:
54 # Parse Cloud Event
55 event = CloudEvent(cloudevent)
56 pubsub_data = event.data
57
58 # Extract Pub/Sub message
59 message_data = pubsub_data['message']
60 message_id = message_data['messageId']
61 publish_time = message_data['publishTime']
62 attributes = message_data.get('attributes', {})
63
64 # Decode message data
65 encoded_data = message_data['data']
66 decoded_data = base64.b64decode(encoded_data).decode('utf-8')
67 event_payload = json.loads(decoded_data)
68
69 # Process based on message attributes
70 event_type = attributes.get('event_type', 'unknown')
71 priority = attributes.get('priority', 'normal')
72
73 # Route to appropriate handler
74 if event_type == 'order_created':
75 result = process_order_created(event_payload, priority)
76 elif event_type == 'payment_received':
77 result = process_payment_received(event_payload, priority)
78 else:
79 result = f"Unhandled event type: {event_type}"
80
81 return f"Processed message {message_id} with event type {event_type}: {result}"
82
83except Exception as e:
84 return f"Error processing Pub/Sub event: {str(e)}"
85
86def handle_user_created(user_id, user_data):
87 """Handle user creation event."""
88 # Send welcome email, create profile, etc.
89 return f"Welcome email sent to user {user_id}"
90
91def handle_user_updated(user_id, user_data):
92 """Handle user update event."""
93 # Update search index, invalidate cache, etc.
94 return f"User profile updated for {user_id}"
95
96def handle_user_deleted(user_id):
97 """Handle user deletion event."""
98 # Clean up user data, revoke tokens, etc.
99 return f"User data cleaned up for {user_id}"
100
101def process_order_created(order_data, priority):
102 """Process order creation with priority handling."""
103 if priority == 'high':
104 # Process high priority orders immediately
105 return f"High priority order {order_data.get('order_id')} processed immediately"
106 else:
107 # Queue normal priority orders
108 return f"Order {order_data.get('order_id')} queued for processing"
109
110def process_payment_received(payment_data, priority):
111 """Process payment received event."""
112 # Update order status, send confirmation, etc.
113 return f"Payment confirmed for order {payment_data.get('order_id')}"
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
3const firestore = new Firestore();
4
5// Process Firestore events using Cloud Events
6functions.cloudEvent('processFirestoreEvent', async (cloudevent) => {
7 try {
8 // Cloud Event data contains the Firestore event
9 const {value} = cloudevent.data;
10 const {name: documentName, fields} = value;
11 const eventType = cloudevent.type;
12
13 // Extract document ID
14 const documentId = documentName.split('/').pop();
15
16 // Convert Firestore fields to JavaScript object
17 const userData = {};
18 Object.keys(fields).forEach(fieldName => {
19 const fieldValue = fields[fieldName];
20 if (fieldValue.stringValue !== undefined) {
21 userData[fieldName] = fieldValue.stringValue;
22 } else if (fieldValue.integerValue !== undefined) {
23 userData[fieldName] = parseInt(fieldValue.integerValue);
24 } else if (fieldValue.booleanValue !== undefined) {
25 userData[fieldName] = fieldValue.booleanValue;
26 }
27 });
28
29 // Process based on event type
30 let result;
31 switch (eventType) {
32 case 'google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.created':
33 result = await handleUserCreated(documentId, userData);
34 break;
35 case 'google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.updated':
36 result = await handleUserUpdated(documentId, userData);
37 break;
38 case 'google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.deleted':
39 result = await handleUserDeleted(documentId);
40 break;
41 default:
42 result = `Unhandled event type: ${eventType}`;
43 }
44
45 console.log(`Processed ${eventType} for user ${documentId}`);
46 return `Processed ${eventType} for user ${documentId}: ${result}`;
47 } catch (error) {
48 console.error('Error processing Firestore event:', error);
49 throw new Error(`Error processing Firestore event: ${error.message}`);
50 }
51});
52
53// Process Pub/Sub events using Cloud Events
54functions.cloudEvent('processPubSubEvent', async (cloudevent) => {
55 try {
56 const {message} = cloudevent.data;
57 const {messageId, data, attributes = {}} = message;
58
59 // Decode message data
60 const decodedData = Buffer.from(data, 'base64').toString();
61 const eventPayload = JSON.parse(decodedData);
62
63 const eventType = attributes.eventType || 'unknown';
64 const priority = attributes.priority || 'normal';
65
66 let result;
67 switch (eventType) {
68 case 'orderCreated':
69 result = await processOrderCreated(eventPayload, priority);
70 break;
71 case 'paymentReceived':
72 result = await processPaymentReceived(eventPayload, priority);
73 break;
74 default:
75 result = `Unhandled event type: ${eventType}`;
76 }
77
78 console.log(`Processed message ${messageId} with event type ${eventType}`);
79 return `Processed message ${messageId}: ${result}`;
80 } catch (error) {
81 console.error('Error processing Pub/Sub event:', error);
82 throw new Error(`Error processing Pub/Sub event: ${error.message}`);
83 }
84});
85
86// Helper functions
87 async function handleUserCreated(userId, userData) {
88 // Send welcome email, create profile
89 return `Welcome email sent to user ${userId}`;
90 }
91
92 async function handleUserUpdated(userId, userData) {
93 // Update search index, invalidate cache
94 return `User profile updated for ${userId}`;
95 }
96
97 async function handleUserDeleted(userId) {
98 // Clean up user data, revoke tokens
99 return `User data cleaned up for ${userId}`;
100 }
101
102 async function processOrderCreated(orderData, priority) {
103 if (priority === 'high') {
104 return `High priority order ${orderData.orderId} processed immediately`;
105 } else {
106 return `Order ${orderData.orderId} queued for processing`;
107 }
108
109 async function processPaymentReceived(paymentData, priority) {
110 return `Payment confirmed for order ${paymentData.orderId}`;
111 }
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/base64"
6 "encoding/json"
7 "fmt"
8 "log"
9 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
10 "github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/event"
11)
12
13type UserData struct {
14 Name string `json:"name"`
15 Email string `json:"email"`
16 Role string `json:"role"`
17}
18
19func ProcessFirestoreEvent(ctx context.Context, e event.Event) error {
20 // Extract Firestore event data from Cloud Event
21 var firestoreData map[string]interface{}
22 if err := e.DataAs(&firestoreData); err != nil {
23 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event data: %v", err)
24 }
25
26 value, ok := firestoreData["value"].(map[string]interface{})
27 if !ok {
28 return fmt.Errorf("value not found in event")
29 }
30
31 documentName, _ := value["name"].(string)
32 fields, ok := value["fields"].(map[string]interface{})
33 if !ok {
34 return fmt.Errorf("fields not found in value")
35 }
36
37 // Extract document ID
38 parts := strings.Split(documentName, "/")
39 documentID := parts[len(parts)-1]
40
41 // Convert Firestore fields to struct
42 userData := UserData{}
43 for fieldName, fieldValue := range fields {
44 fieldMap, ok := fieldValue.(map[string]interface{})
45 if !ok {
46 continue
47 }
48
49 switch fieldName {
50 case "name":
51 if val, ok := fieldMap["stringValue"].(string); ok {
52 userData.Name = val
53 }
54 case "email":
55 if val, ok := fieldMap["stringValue"].(string); ok {
56 userData.Email = val
57 }
58 case "role":
59 if val, ok := fieldMap["stringValue"].(string); ok {
60 userData.Role = val
61 }
62 }
63 }
64
65 // Process based on event type
66 var result string
67 switch e.Type() {
68 case "google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.created":
69 result = handleUserCreated(documentID, userData)
70 case "google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.updated":
71 result = handleUserUpdated(documentID, userData)
72 case "google.cloud.firestore.document.v1.deleted":
73 result = handleUserDeleted(documentID)
74 default:
75 result = fmt.Sprintf("Unhandled event type: %s", e.Type())
76 }
77
78 log.Printf("Processed %s event for user %s: %s", e.Type(), documentID, result)
79 return nil
80}
81
82func ProcessPubSubEvent(ctx context.Context, e event.Event) error {
83 // Extract Pub/Sub message from Cloud Event
84 var pubsubData map[string]interface{}
85 if err := e.DataAs(&pubsubData); err != nil {
86 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event data: %v", err)
87 }
88
89 message, ok := pubsubData["message"].(map[string]interface{})
90 if !ok {
91 return fmt.Errorf("message not found in event")
92 }
93
94 messageId, _ := message["messageId"].(string)
95 data, ok := message["data"].(string)
96 if !ok {
97 return fmt.Errorf("data not found in message")
98 }
99
100 // Decode base64 data
101 decodedData, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(data)
102 if err != nil {
103 return fmt.Errorf("failed to decode message data: %v", err)
104 }
105
106 // Parse JSON payload
107 var eventPayload map[string]interface{}
108 if err := json.Unmarshal(decodedData, &eventPayload); err != nil {
109 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event payload: %v", err)
110 }
111
112 attributes, _ := message["attributes"].(map[string]interface{})
113 eventType, _ := attributes["eventType"].(string)
114 priority, _ := attributes["priority"].(string)
115
116 if eventType == "" {
117 eventType = "unknown"
118 }
119 if priority == "" {
120 priority = "normal"
121 }
122
123 var result string
124 switch eventType {
125 case "orderCreated":
126 result = processOrderCreated(eventPayload, priority)
127 case "paymentReceived":
128 result = processPaymentReceived(eventPayload, priority)
129 default:
130 result = fmt.Sprintf("Unhandled event type: %s", eventType)
131 }
132
133 log.Printf("Processed message %s with event type %s: %s", messageId, eventType, result)
134 return nil
135}
136
137// Helper functions
138func handleUserCreated(userID string, userData UserData) string {
139 // Send welcome email, create profile
140 return fmt.Sprintf("Welcome email sent to user %s", userID)
141 }
142
143func handleUserUpdated(userID string, userData UserData) string {
144 // Update search index, invalidate cache
145 return fmt.Sprintf("User profile updated for %s", userID)
146 }
147
148func handleUserDeleted(userID string) string {
149 // Clean up user data, revoke tokens
150 return fmt.Sprintf("User data cleaned up for %s", userID)
151 }
152
153func processOrderCreated(orderData map[string]interface{}, priority string) string {
154 orderId, _ := orderData["orderId"].(string)
155 if priority == "high" {
156 return fmt.Sprintf("High priority order %s processed immediately", orderId)
157 }
158 return fmt.Sprintf("Order %s queued for processing", orderId)
159 }
160
161func processPaymentReceived(paymentData map[string]interface{}, priority string) string {
162 orderId, _ := paymentData["orderId"].(string)
163 return fmt.Sprintf("Payment confirmed for order %s", orderId)
164 }
📡 Cloud Events Best Practices
  • Standardization: Use Cloud Events specification for consistent event handling across platforms
  • Event Types: Define clear, descriptive event type names following the Cloud Events specification
  • Data Structure: Include essential metadata in event attributes for filtering and routing
  • Versioning: Use event versioning to maintain backward compatibility
  • Validation: Validate event data structure and required fields
  • Error Handling: Implement proper error handling for malformed or unexpected events
📨

Pub/Sub Integration

Advanced

Cloud Pub/Sub enables reliable, asynchronous messaging between Cloud Functions and other Google Cloud services or external systems. It's ideal for decoupling services and building event-driven architectures.

📨 Pub/Sub Message Flow: Publisher → Topic → Subscription → Function
📤 Publisher
Send Message
📋 Topic
Message Queue
📨 Subscription
Pull Messages
⚡ Function
Process Message
Asynchronous Message Processing Architecture
Pub/Sub Message Publisher
python
1import json
2import base64
3from google.cloud import pubsub_v1
4from google.cloud import functions_framework
5from flask import request, jsonify
6
7 publisher = pubsub_v1.PublisherClient()
8topic_path = publisher.topic_path('your-project-id', 'user-events')
9
10@functions_framework.http
11def publish_user_event(request):
12 """Publish user events to Pub/Sub topic."""
13 if request.method != 'POST':
14 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
15
16 data = request.get_json()
17 user_id = data.get('user_id')
18 event_type = data.get('event_type')
19 event_data = data.get('event_data', {})
20
21 if not all([user_id, event_type]):
22 return jsonify({'error': 'user_id and event_type are required'}), 400
23
24 try:
25 # Create message payload
26 message = {
27 'user_id': user_id,
28 'event_type': event_type,
29 'event_data': event_data,
30 'timestamp': str(datetime.utcnow()),
31 'source': 'user_api'
32 }
33
34 # Convert to JSON and encode
35 message_json = json.dumps(message)
36 message_bytes = message_json.encode('utf-8')
37
38 # Add attributes for filtering
39 attributes = {
40 'event_type': event_type,
41 'user_id': user_id,
42 'priority': data.get('priority', 'normal')
43 }
44
45 # Publish message
46future = publisher.publish(topic_path, message_bytes, attributes)
47 message_id = future.result()
48
49 return jsonify({
50 'message_id': message_id,
51 'status': 'published',
52 'topic': topic_path
53 })
54
55except Exception as e:
56 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500
javascript
1const {PubSub} = require('@google-cloud/pubsub');
2const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
3
4const pubsub = new PubSub();
5const topicName = 'user-events';
6
7// Publish user events to Pub/Sub
8functions.http('publishUserEvent', async (req, res) => {
9 try {
10 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
11 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
12 }
13
14 const { userId, eventType, eventData, priority = 'normal' } = req.body;
15
16 if (!userId || !eventType) {
17 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'userId and eventType are required' });
18 }
19
20 // Create message payload
21 const message = {
22 userId,
23 eventType,
24 eventData: eventData || {},
25 timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
26 source: 'user_api'
27 };
28
29 // Get topic
30 const topic = pubsub.topic(topicName);
31
32 // Publish message with attributes
33 const messageId = await topic.publishMessage({
34 data: Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(message)),
35 attributes: {
36 eventType,
37 userId,
38 priority
39 }
40 });
41
42 res.json({
43 messageId,
44 status: 'published',
45 topic: topicName
46 });
47 } catch (error) {
48 console.error('Publish error:', error);
49 res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
50 }
51});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "fmt"
7 "net/http"
8 "time"
9 "cloud.google.com/go/pubsub"
10)
11
12type PublishRequest struct {
13 UserID string `json:"userId"`
14 EventType string `json:"eventType"`
15 EventData map[string]interface{} `json:"eventData"`
16 Priority string `json:"priority"`
17}
18
19func PublishUserEvent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
20 if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
21 http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
22 return
23 }
24
25 var req PublishRequest
26 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
27 http.Error(w, "Invalid JSON", http.StatusBadRequest)
28 return
29 }
30
31 if req.UserID == "" || req.EventType == "" {
32 http.Error(w, "userId and eventType are required", http.StatusBadRequest)
33 return
34 }
35
36 if req.Priority == "" {
37 req.Priority = "normal"
38 }
39
40 ctx := context.Background()
41 client, err := pubsub.NewClient(ctx, "")
42 if err != nil {
43 fmt.Printf("Failed to create Pub/Sub client: %v\n", err)
44 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
45 return
46 }
47 defer client.Close()
48
49 // Create message payload
50 message := map[string]interface{}{
51 "userId": req.UserID,
52 "eventType": req.EventType,
53 "eventData": req.EventData,
54 "timestamp": time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
55 "source": "user_api",
56 }
57
58 messageJSON, err := json.Marshal(message)
59 if err != nil {
60 http.Error(w, "Failed to marshal message", http.StatusInternalServerError)
61 return
62 }
63
64 // Get topic and publish
65 topic := client.Topic("user-events")
66 result := topic.Publish(ctx, &pubsub.Message{
67 Data: messageJSON,
68 Attributes: map[string]string{
69 "eventType": req.EventType,
70 "userId": req.UserID,
71 "priority": req.Priority,
72 },
73 })
74
75 messageID, err := result.Get(ctx)
76 if err != nil {
77 http.Error(w, "Failed to publish message", http.StatusInternalServerError)
78 return
79 }
80
81 response := map[string]string{
82 "messageId": messageID,
83 "status": "published",
84 "topic": "user-events",
85 }
86
87 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
88 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
89}
Pub/Sub Message Consumer
python
1import json
2import base64
3from google.cloud import firestore
4from google.events.cloud.pubsub import MessagePublishedData
5from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent
6
7firestore_client = firestore.Client()
8
9def process_user_events(cloudevent):
10 """Process user events from Pub/Sub using Cloud Events."""
11 try:
12 # Parse Cloud Event
13 event = CloudEvent(cloudevent)
14 pubsub_data = event.data
15
16 # Extract Pub/Sub message
17 message_data = pubsub_data['message']
18 message_attributes = message_data.get('attributes', {})
19 message_id = message_data['messageId']
20 publish_time = message_data['publishTime']
21
22 # Decode message data
23 encoded_data = message_data['data']
24 decoded_data = base64.b64decode(encoded_data).decode('utf-8')
25 event_payload = json.loads(decoded_data)
26
27 # Extract event details
28 user_id = event_payload['user_id']
29 event_type = event_payload['event_type']
30 event_data = event_payload.get('event_data', {})
31 source = event_payload.get('source', 'unknown')
32
33 # Process different event types
34 if event_type == 'user_registered':
35 # Send welcome email
36 result = send_welcome_email(user_id, event_data)
37 elif event_type == 'profile_updated':
38 # Update search index
39 result = update_search_index(user_id, event_data)
40 elif event_type == 'user_login':
41 # Log user activity
42 result = log_user_activity(user_id, 'login', event_data)
43 else:
44 result = f"Unhandled event type: {event_type}"
45
46 # Log processing result
47 log_event_processing(message_id, user_id, event_type, result)
48
49 return f"Processed {event_type} event for user {user_id}"
50
51except Exception as e:
52 return f"Error processing message {message_id}: {str(e)}"
53
54def send_welcome_email(user_id, event_data):
55 """Send welcome email to new user."""
56 # Implementation for sending welcome email
57 return f"Welcome email sent to user {user_id}"
58
59def update_search_index(user_id, event_data):
60 """Update user search index."""
61 # Implementation for updating search index
62 return f"Search index updated for user {user_id}"
63
64def log_user_activity(user_id, action, event_data):
65 """Log user activity."""
66 # Implementation for logging user activity
67 return f"Activity logged for user {user_id}: {action}"
68
69def log_event_processing(message_id, user_id, event_type, result):
70 """Log event processing result."""
71 # Store processing log in Firestore
72 doc_ref = firestore_client.collection('event_logs').document()
73 doc_ref.set({
74 'message_id': message_id,
75 'user_id': user_id,
76 'event_type': event_type,
77 'result': result,
78 'processed_at': firestore.SERVER_TIMESTAMP
79 })
javascript
1const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
2const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
3const firestore = new Firestore();
4
5// Process user events from Pub/Sub
6functions.cloudEvent('processUserEvents', async (cloudevent) => {
7 try {
8 // Extract Pub/Sub message data
9 const {message} = cloudevent.data;
10 const {messageId, publishTime, data, attributes} = message;
11
12 // Decode message data
13 const decodedData = Buffer.from(data, 'base64').toString();
14 const eventPayload = JSON.parse(decodedData);
15
16 // Extract event details
17 const {userId, eventType, eventData, source = 'unknown'} = eventPayload;
18
19 // Process different event types
20 let result;
21 switch (eventType) {
22 case 'userRegistered':
23 result = await sendWelcomeEmail(userId, eventData);
24 break;
25 case 'profileUpdated':
26 result = await updateSearchIndex(userId, eventData);
27 break;
28 case 'userLogin':
29 result = await logUserActivity(userId, 'login', eventData);
30 break;
31 default:
32 result = `Unhandled event type: ${eventType}`;
33 }
34
35 // Log processing result
36 await logEventProcessing(messageId, userId, eventType, result);
37
38 console.log(`Processed ${eventType} event for user ${userId}`);
39 return `Processed ${eventType} event for user ${userId}`;
40 } catch (error) {
41 console.error('Error processing message:', error);
42 throw new Error(`Error processing message ${messageId}: ${error.message}`);
43 }
44});
45
46// Helper functions
47 async function sendWelcomeEmail(userId, eventData) {
48 // Implementation for sending welcome email
49 return `Welcome email sent to user ${userId}`;
50 }
51
52 async function updateSearchIndex(userId, eventData) {
53 // Implementation for updating search index
54 return `Search index updated for user ${userId}`;
55 }
56
57 async function logUserActivity(userId, action, eventData) {
58 // Implementation for logging user activity
59 return `Activity logged for user ${userId}: ${action}`;
60 }
61
62 async function logEventProcessing(messageId, userId, eventType, result) {
63 // Store processing log in Firestore
64 const docRef = firestore.collection('eventLogs').doc();
65 await docRef.set({
66 messageId,
67 userId,
68 eventType,
69 result,
70 processedAt: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp()
71 });
72 }
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/base64"
6 "encoding/json"
7 "fmt"
8 "log"
9 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
10 "github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/event"
11)
12
13type EventPayload struct {
14 UserID string `json:"userId"`
15 EventType string `json:"eventType"`
16 EventData map[string]interface{} `json:"eventData"`
17 Source string `json:"source"`
18}
19
20func ProcessUserEvents(ctx context.Context, e event.Event) error {
21 // Extract Pub/Sub message data
22 var pubsubData map[string]interface{}
23 if err := e.DataAs(&pubsubData); err != nil {
24 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event data: %v", err)
25 }
26
27 message, ok := pubsubData["message"].(map[string]interface{})
28 if !ok {
29 return fmt.Errorf("message not found in event")
30 }
31
32 messageId, _ := message["messageId"].(string)
33 data, ok := message["data"].(string)
34 if !ok {
35 return fmt.Errorf("data not found in message")
36 }
37
38 // Decode base64 data
39 decodedData, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(data)
40 if err != nil {
41 return fmt.Errorf("failed to decode message data: %v", err)
42 }
43
44 // Parse JSON payload
45 var payload EventPayload
46 if err := json.Unmarshal(decodedData, &payload); err != nil {
47 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event payload: %v", err)
48 }
49
50 if payload.Source == "" {
51 payload.Source = "unknown"
52 }
53
54 // Process different event types
55 var result string
56 switch payload.EventType {
57 case "userRegistered":
58 result = sendWelcomeEmail(payload.UserID, payload.EventData)
59 case "profileUpdated":
60 result = updateSearchIndex(payload.UserID, payload.EventData)
61 case "userLogin":
62 result = logUserActivity(payload.UserID, "login", payload.EventData)
63 default:
64 result = fmt.Sprintf("Unhandled event type: %s", payload.EventType)
65 }
66
67 // Log processing result
68 logEventProcessing(messageId, payload.UserID, payload.EventType, result)
69
70 log.Printf("Processed %s event for user %s", payload.EventType, payload.UserID)
71 return nil
72}
73
74// Helper functions
75func sendWelcomeEmail(userID string, eventData map[string]interface{}) string {
76 // Implementation for sending welcome email
77 return fmt.Sprintf("Welcome email sent to user %s", userID)
78 }
79
80func updateSearchIndex(userID string, eventData map[string]interface{}) string {
81 // Implementation for updating search index
82 return fmt.Sprintf("Search index updated for user %s", userID)
83 }
84
85func logUserActivity(userID, action string, eventData map[string]interface{}) string {
86 // Implementation for logging user activity
87 return fmt.Sprintf("Activity logged for user %s: %s", userID, action)
88 }
89
90func logEventProcessing(messageId, userID, eventType, result string) {
91 // Store processing log in Firestore
92 ctx := context.Background()
93 client, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, "")
94 if err != nil {
95 log.Printf("Failed to create Firestore client: %v", err)
