96 return
97 }
98 defer client.Close()
99
100 docRef := client.Collection("eventLogs").NewDoc()
101 _, err = docRef.Set(ctx, map[string]interface{}{
102 "messageId": messageId,
103 "userId": userID,
104 "eventType": eventType,
105 "result": result,
106 "processedAt": firestore.ServerTimestamp,
107 })
108 if err != nil {
109 log.Printf("Failed to log event processing: %v", err)
110 }
111 }
📨 Pub/Sub Best Practices
  • Message Ordering: Don't rely on message ordering; design for eventual consistency
  • Idempotency: Make message processing idempotent to handle duplicates
  • Dead Letter Topics: Configure dead letter topics for failed message processing
  • Message Attributes: Use attributes for filtering and routing messages
  • Batch Processing: Consider batch processing for high-throughput scenarios
  • Monitoring: Monitor message processing latency and error rates
📈

Scaling & Concurrency

Advanced

Cloud Functions automatically scale based on incoming requests, but understanding scaling behavior and concurrency limits is crucial for building high-performance applications.

Concurrency Management
python
1import asyncio
2import threading
3import concurrent.futures
4from google.cloud import firestore
5from google.cloud import storage
6from flask import jsonify
7import functions_framework
8
9 firestore_client = firestore.Client()
10 storage_client = storage.Client()
11
12@functions_framework.http
13def concurrent_processing(request):
14 """Demonstrate concurrent processing within function limits."""
15 if request.method != 'POST':
16 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
17
18 data = request.get_json()
19 user_ids = data.get('user_ids', [])
20 max_concurrent = min(data.get('max_concurrent', 5), 10) # Respect limits
21
22 if not user_ids:
23 return jsonify({'error': 'user_ids list is required'}), 400
24
25 try:
26 # Process users concurrently with ThreadPoolExecutor
27 with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_concurrent) as executor:
28 # Submit all tasks
29 future_to_user = {executor.submit(process_user_data, user_id): user_id for user_id in user_ids}
30
31 results = []
32 for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_user):
33 user_id = future_to_user[future]
34 try:
35 result = future.result()
36 results.append({'user_id': user_id, 'status': 'success', 'data': result})
37 except Exception as exc:
38 results.append({'user_id': user_id, 'status': 'error', 'error': str(exc)})
39
40 return jsonify({
41 'total_users': len(user_ids),
42 'processed_concurrently': max_concurrent,
43 'results': results
44 })
45
46except Exception as e:
47 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500
48
49def process_user_data(user_id):
50 """Process individual user data."""
51 # Simulate processing time
52 import time
53 time.sleep(0.1) # Simulate I/O operation
54
55 # Get user profile from Firestore
56 user_doc = firestore_client.collection('users').document(user_id).get()
57 if user_doc.exists:
58 user_data = user_doc.to_dict()
59 return {
60 'name': user_data.get('name'),
61 'email': user_data.get('email'),
62 'last_login': user_data.get('last_login')
63 }
64 else:
65 raise ValueError(f"User {user_id} not found")
javascript
1const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
2const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
3
4const firestore = new Firestore();
5
6// Concurrent processing with controlled concurrency
7functions.http('concurrentProcessing', async (req, res) => {
8 try {
9 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
10 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
11 }
12
13 const { userIds, maxConcurrent = 5 } = req.body;
14 const limitedConcurrency = Math.min(maxConcurrent, 10); // Respect function limits
15
16 if (!userIds || !Array.isArray(userIds)) {
17 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'userIds array is required' });
18 }
19
20 // Process users with controlled concurrency
21 const results = [];
22 const semaphore = new Array(limitedConcurrency).fill(null);
23
24 for (let i = 0; i < userIds.length; i +=limitedConcurrency) {
25 const batch = userIds.slice(i, i + limitedConcurrency);
26 const batchPromises = batch.map(userId => processUserData(userId));
27 const batchResults = await Promise.allSettled(batchPromises);
28
29 batchResults.forEach((result, index) => {
30 const userId = batch[index];
31 if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
32 results.push({ userId, status: 'success', data: result.value });
33 } else {
34 results.push({ userId, status: 'error', error: result.reason.message });
35 }
36 });
37 }
38
39 res.json({
40 totalUsers: userIds.length,
41 processedConcurrently: limitedConcurrency,
42 results
43 });
44 } catch (error) {
45 console.error('Concurrent processing error:', error);
46 res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
47 }
48});
49
50// Process individual user data
51 async function processUserData(userId) {
52 // Simulate processing delay
53 await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 100));
54
55 // Get user data from Firestore
56 const userDoc = await firestore.collection('users').doc(userId).get();
57 if (userDoc.exists) {
58 const userData = userDoc.data();
59 return {
60 name: userData.name,
61 email: userData.email,
62 lastLogin: userData.lastLogin
63 };
64 } else {
65 throw new Error(`User ${userId} not found`);
66 }
67 }
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "fmt"
7 "net/http"
8 "sync"
9 "time"
10 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
11)
12
13type ConcurrentRequest struct {
14 UserIDs []string `json:"userIds"`
15 MaxConcurrent int `json:"maxConcurrent"`
16}
17
18type ProcessingResult struct {
19 UserID string `json:"userId"`
20 Status string `json:"status"`
21 Data map[string]interface{} `json:"data,omitempty"`
22 Error string `json:"error,omitempty"`
23}
24
25func ConcurrentProcessing(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
26 if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
27 http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
28 return
29 }
30
31 var req ConcurrentRequest
32 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
33 http.Error(w, "Invalid JSON", http.StatusBadRequest)
34 return
35 }
36
37 if req.MaxConcurrent <= 0 {
38 req.MaxConcurrent = 5
39 }
40 if req.MaxConcurrent > 10 {
41 req.MaxConcurrent = 10 // Respect function limits
42 }
43
44 ctx := context.Background()
45 client, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, "")
46 if err != nil {
47 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
48 return
49 }
50 defer client.Close()
51
52 // Process users concurrently with semaphore
53 semaphore := make(chan struct{}, req.MaxConcurrent)
54 resultsChan := make(chan ProcessingResult, len(req.UserIDs))
55
56 var wg sync.WaitGroup
57
58 // Start goroutines for each user
59 for _, userID := range req.UserIDs {
60 wg.Add(1)
61 go func(uid string) {
62 defer wg.Done()
63 semaphore <- struct{}{} // Acquire
64 defer func() { <-semaphore }() // Release
65
66 result := processUserData(ctx, client, uid)
67 resultsChan <- result
68 }(userID)
69 }
70
71 // Close results channel when all goroutines complete
72 go func() {
73 wg.Wait()
74 close(resultsChan)
75 }()
76
77 // Collect results
78 results := []ProcessingResult{}
79 for result := range resultsChan {
80 results = append(results, result)
81 }
82
83 response := map[string]interface{}{
84 "totalUsers": len(req.UserIDs),
85 "processedConcurrently": req.MaxConcurrent,
86 "results": results,
87 }
88
89 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
90 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
91}
92
93func processUserData(ctx context.Context, client *firestore.Client, userID string) ProcessingResult {
94 // Simulate processing time
95 time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
96
97 // Get user data from Firestore
98 doc, err := client.Collection("users").Doc(userID).Get(ctx)
99 if err != nil {
100 return ProcessingResult{
101 UserID: userID,
102 Status: "error",
103 Error: fmt.Sprintf("User not found: %v", err),
104 }
105 }
106
107 var userData map[string]interface{}
108 if err := doc.DataTo(&userData); err != nil {
109 return ProcessingResult{
110 UserID: userID,
111 Status: "error",
112 Error: err.Error(),
113 }
114 }
115
116 return ProcessingResult{
117 UserID: userID,
118 Status: "success",
119 Data: userData,
120 }
121 }
⚡ Scaling & Concurrency Best Practices
  • Concurrency Limits: Respect function memory and CPU limits when setting concurrent operations
  • Resource Management: Use semaphores or connection pools to control resource usage
  • Error Handling: Handle partial failures gracefully in concurrent operations
  • Monitoring: Monitor function performance and adjust concurrency based on load
  • Timeouts: Set appropriate timeouts for concurrent operations
  • Cost Optimization: Balance performance gains with increased resource consumption
📊

Monitoring & Logging

Advanced

Effective monitoring and logging are crucial for maintaining reliable Cloud Functions. Cloud Logging and Cloud Monitoring provide comprehensive observability into function performance, errors, and usage patterns.

Structured Logging & Error Tracking
python
1import logging
2import time
3from functools import wraps
4from google.cloud import logging as cloud_logging
5from flask import request, jsonify
6import functions_framework
7
8 # Initialize Cloud Logging
9 cloud_logging.Client().setup_logging()
10
11 logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
12 logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
13
14def performance_monitor(func):
15 """Decorator to monitor function performance."""
16 @wraps(func)
17 def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
18 start_time = time.time()
19 request_id = request.headers.get('X-Request-ID', 'unknown')
20
21 logger.info(f"Function {func.__name__} started", extra={
22 'request_id': request_id,
23 'function_name': func.__name__,
24 'event_type': 'function_start'
25 })
26
27 try:
28 result = func(*args, **kwargs)
29 execution_time = time.time() - start_time
30
31 logger.info(f"Function {func.__name__} completed successfully", extra={
32 'request_id': request_id,
33 'function_name': func.__name__,
34 'execution_time': execution_time,
35 'event_type': 'function_success'
36 })
37
38 return result
39
40except Exception as e:
41 execution_time = time.time() - start_time
42 logger.error(f"Function {func.__name__} failed", extra={
43 'request_id': request_id,
44 'function_name': func.__name__,
45 'execution_time': execution_time,
46 'error_message': str(e),
47 'event_type': 'function_error'
48 }, exc_info=True)
49 raise
50 return wrapper
51
52@functions_framework.http
53@performance_monitor
54def monitored_function(request):
55 """Example function with comprehensive monitoring."""
56 user_id = request.args.get('user_id')
57 action = request.args.get('action', 'default')
58
59 # Structured logging with context
60 logger.info(f"Processing action: {action} for user: {user_id}", extra={
61 'user_id': user_id,
62 'action': action,
63 'user_agent': request.headers.get('User-Agent'),
64 'ip_address': request.remote_addr,
65 'event_type': 'user_action'
66 })
67
68 # Simulate processing with potential errors
69 if action == 'error':
70 logger.warning(f"Simulated error for user: {user_id}", extra={
71 'user_id': user_id,
72 'error_type': 'simulated',
73 'event_type': 'error_simulated'
74 })
75 raise ValueError("Simulated processing error")
76
77 # Simulate successful processing
78 time.sleep(0.05) # Simulate processing time
79
80 logger.info(f"Successfully processed action: {action} for user: {user_id}", extra={
81 'user_id': user_id,
82 'action': action,
83 'processing_time': 0.05,
84 'event_type': 'processing_complete'
85 })
86
87 return jsonify({
88 'user_id': user_id,
89 'action': action,
90 'status': 'completed',
91 'timestamp': time.time()
92 })
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Logging} = require('@google-cloud/logging');
3
4const logging = new Logging();
5const log = logging.log('cloud-functions');
6
7// Performance monitoring decorator
8 function performanceMonitor(target, name, descriptor) {
9 const originalMethod = descriptor.value;
10 descriptor.value = async function(...args) {
11 const startTime = Date.now();
12 const requestId = this.req?.headers?.['x-request-id'] || 'unknown';
13
14 // Log function start
15 const metadata = {
16 resource: { type: 'cloud_function' },
17 labels: {
18 function_name: name,
19 request_id: requestId,
20 event_type: 'function_start'
21 }
22 };
23 await log.write(log.entry(metadata, { message: `Function ${name} started` }));
24
25 try {
26 const result = await originalMethod.apply(this, args);
27 const executionTime = Date.now() - startTime;
28
29 // Log success
30 const successMetadata = {
31 resource: { type: 'cloud_function' },
32 labels: {
33 function_name: name,
34 request_id: requestId,
35 execution_time: executionTime,
36 event_type: 'function_success'
37 }
38 };
39 await log.write(log.entry(successMetadata, { message: `Function ${name} completed successfully`, executionTime }));
40
41 return result;
42 } catch (error) {
43 const executionTime = Date.now() - startTime;
44
45 // Log error
46 const errorMetadata = {
47 resource: { type: 'cloud_function' },
48 severity: 'ERROR',
49 labels: {
50 function_name: name,
51 request_id: requestId,
52 execution_time: executionTime,
53 error_message: error.message,
54 event_type: 'function_error'
55 }
56 };
57 await log.write(log.entry(errorMetadata, { message: `Function ${name} failed`, error: error.message }));
58 throw error;
59 }
60 };
61 return descriptor;
62 }
63
64class MonitoredFunction {
65 constructor(req, res) {
66 this.req = req;
67 this.res = res;
68 }
69
70 @performanceMonitor
71 async execute() {
72 const userId = this.req.query.userId;
73 const action = this.req.query.action || 'default';
74
75 // Structured logging
76 const logEntry = {
77 message: `Processing action: ${action} for user: ${userId}`,
78 userId,
79 action,
80 userAgent: this.req.headers['user-agent'],
81 ipAddress: this.req.ip,
82 eventType: 'user_action'
83 };
84 await log.write(log.entry({
85 resource: { type: 'cloud_function' },
86 labels: { event_type: 'user_action' }
87 }, logEntry));
88
89 if (action === 'error') {
90 await log.write(log.entry({
91 severity: 'WARNING',
92 labels: { event_type: 'error_simulated' }
93 }, { message: `Simulated error for user: ${userId}`, userId, errorType: 'simulated' }));
94 throw new Error('Simulated processing error');
95 }
96
97 // Simulate processing
98 await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 50));
99
100 await log.write(log.entry({
101 labels: { event_type: 'processing_complete' }
102 }, { message: `Successfully processed action: ${action} for user: ${userId}`, userId, action, processingTime: 50 }));
103
104 this.res.json({
105 userId,
106 action,
107 status: 'completed',
108 timestamp: Date.now()
109 });
110 }
111 }
112
113const monitoredFunction = new MonitoredFunction();
114functions.http('monitoredFunction', (req, res) => {
115 monitoredFunction.req = req;
116 monitoredFunction.res = res;
117 monitoredFunction.execute();
118 });
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "log"
7 "net/http"
8 "time"
9 "go.uber.org/zap"
10 "github.com/google/uuid"
11)
12
13var logger *zap.Logger
14
15func init() {
16 var err error
17 logger, err = zap.NewProduction()
18 if err != nil {
19 log.Fatal(err)
20 }
21 }
22
23type LogEntry struct {
24 Message string `json:"message"`
25 UserID string `json:"userId,omitempty"`
26 Action string `json:"action,omitempty"`
27 UserAgent string `json:"userAgent,omitempty"`
28 IPAddress string `json:"ipAddress,omitempty"`
29 EventType string `json:"eventType"`
30 ExecutionTime float64 `json:"executionTime,omitempty"`
31 ErrorMessage string `json:"errorMessage,omitempty"`
32 RequestID string `json:"requestId"`
33 FunctionName string `json:"functionName"`
34}
35
36func performanceMonitor(next http.HandlerFunc, functionName string) http.HandlerFunc {
37 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
38 startTime := time.Now()
39 requestID := r.Header.Get("X-Request-ID")
40 if requestID == "" {
41 requestID = uuid.New().String()
42 }
43
44 // Log function start
45 logger.Info("Function started",
46 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
47 zap.String("functionName", functionName),
48 zap.String("eventType", "function_start"))
49
50 defer func() {
51 if r := recover(); r != nil {
52 executionTime := time.Since(startTime).Seconds()
53 logger.Error("Function panicked",
54 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
55 zap.String("functionName", functionName),
56 zap.Float64("executionTime", executionTime),
57 zap.Any("panic", r),
58 zap.String("eventType", "function_panic"))
59 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
60 }
61 }()
62
63 try {
64 next(w, r)
65 executionTime := time.Since(startTime).Seconds()
66
67 // Log success
68 logger.Info("Function completed successfully",
69 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
70 zap.String("functionName", functionName),
71 zap.Float64("executionTime", executionTime),
72 zap.String("eventType", "function_success"))
73 } catch {
74 executionTime := time.Since(startTime).Seconds()
75
76 // Log error
77 logger.Error("Function failed",
78 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
79 zap.String("functionName", functionName),
80 zap.Float64("executionTime", executionTime),
81 zap.Error(err),
82 zap.String("eventType", "function_error"))
83 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
84 }
85 }
86 }
87}
88
89func MonitoredFunction(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
90 userID := r.URL.Query().Get("userId")
91 action := r.URL.Query().Get("action")
92 if action == "" {
93 action = "default"
94 }
95
96 requestID := r.Header.Get("X-Request-ID")
97 if requestID == "" {
98 requestID = uuid.New().String()
99 }
100
101 // Structured logging
102 logger.Info("Processing user action",
103 zap.String("message", fmt.Sprintf("Processing action: %s for user: %s", action, userID)),
104 zap.String("userId", userID),
105 zap.String("action", action),
106 zap.String("userAgent", r.Header.Get("User-Agent")),
107 zap.String("ipAddress", r.RemoteAddr),
108 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
109 zap.String("functionName", "MonitoredFunction"),
110 zap.String("eventType", "user_action"))
111
112 if action == "error" {
113 logger.Warn("Simulated error",
114 zap.String("userId", userID),
115 zap.String("errorType", "simulated"),
116 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
117 zap.String("eventType", "error_simulated"))
118 http.Error(w, "Simulated processing error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
119 return
120 }
121
122 // Simulate processing
123 time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
124
125 logger.Info("Processing completed",
126 zap.String("message", fmt.Sprintf("Successfully processed action: %s for user: %s", action, userID)),
127 zap.String("userId", userID),
128 zap.String("action", action),
129 zap.Float64("processingTime", 0.05),
130 zap.String("requestId", requestID),
131 zap.String("functionName", "MonitoredFunction"),
132 zap.String("eventType", "processing_complete"))
133
134 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
135 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string interface{}{
136 "userId": userID,
137 "action": action,
138 "status": "completed",
139 "timestamp": time.Now().Unix(),
140 })
141 }
📊 Monitoring & Logging Best Practices
  • Structured Logging: Use consistent log formats with contextual information
  • Performance Monitoring: Track execution time, memory usage, and error rates
  • Error Tracking: Log errors with stack traces and contextual data
  • Request Tracing: Use request IDs to trace requests across services
  • Log Levels: Use appropriate log levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR)
  • Alerting: Set up alerts for critical errors and performance degradation
🔒

Security & IAM

Advanced

Security is paramount for Cloud Functions. Implementing proper authentication, authorization, and secure coding practices protects your applications and data from unauthorized access and potential threats.

Authentication & Authorization
python
1import jwt
2import functools
3from datetime import datetime, timedelta
4from flask import request, jsonify, g
5from google.auth.transport import requests
6from google.oauth2 import id_token
7import functions_framework
8
9JWT_SECRET = 'your-jwt-secret'
10GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = 'your-google-client-id'
11
12def require_auth(f):
13 """Decorator to require authentication."""
14 @functools.wraps(f)
15 def decorated_function(*args, **kwargs):
16 auth_header = request.headers.get('Authorization')
17 if not auth_header or not auth_header.startswith('Bearer '):
18 return jsonify({'error': 'Missing or invalid authorization header'}), 401
19
20 token = auth_header.split(' ')[1]
21 try:
22 payload = jwt.decode(token, JWT_SECRET, algorithms=['HS256'])
23 if datetime.utcnow().timestamp() > payload['exp']:
24 return jsonify({'error': 'Token expired'}), 401
25 g.user_id = payload['user_id']
26 g.user_role = payload.get('role', 'user')
27 except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
28 return jsonify({'error': 'Token expired'}), 401
29 except jwt.InvalidTokenError:
30 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid token'}), 401
31 return f(*args, **kwargs)
32 return decorated_function
33
34def require_role(required_role):
35 """Decorator to require specific role."""
36 def decorator(f):
37 @functools.wraps(f)
38 def decorated_function(*args, **kwargs):
39 if not hasattr(g, 'user_role') or g.user_role != required_role:
40 return jsonify({'error': 'Insufficient permissions'}), 403
41 return f(*args, **kwargs)
42 return decorated_function
43 return decorator
44
45@functions_framework.http
46def google_auth(request):
47 """Authenticate using Google ID token."""
48 if request.method != 'POST':
49 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
50
51 data = request.get_json()
52 id_token_value = data.get('id_token')
53
54 if not id_token_value:
55 return jsonify({'error': 'ID token is required'}), 400
56
57 try:
58 # Verify Google ID token
59 id_info = id_token.verify_oauth2_token(id_token_value, requests.Request(), GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID)
60
61 # Create JWT token for our application
62 payload = {
63 'user_id': id_info['sub'],
64 'email': id_info['email'],
65 'name': id_info.get('name', ''),
66 'role': 'user',
67 'exp': datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=24)
68 }
69 token = jwt.encode(payload, JWT_SECRET, algorithm='HS256')
70
71 return jsonify({
72 'token': token,
73 'user': {
74 'id': id_info['sub'],
75 'email': id_info['email'],
76 'name': id_info.get('name', '')
77 }
78 })
79
80except Exception as e:
81 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid ID token'}), 401
82
83@functions_framework.http
84@require_auth
85@require_role('admin')
86def admin_only_endpoint(request):
87 """Admin-only endpoint with authentication and authorization."""
88 return jsonify({
89 'message': 'Admin access granted',
90 'user_id': g.user_id,
91 'user_role': g.user_role
92 })
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
3const {OAuth2Client} = require('google-auth-library');
4
5const JWT_SECRET = 'your-jwt-secret';
6const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = 'your-google-client-id';
7const oauth2Client = new OAuth2Client(GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID);
8
9// Authentication middleware
10 function requireAuth(req, res, next) {
11 const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;
12 if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith('Bearer ')) {
13 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Missing or invalid authorization header' });
14 }
15
16 const token = authHeader.split(' ')[1];
17 try {
18 const decoded = jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET);
19 if (Date.now() >= decoded.exp * 1000) {
20 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Token expired' });
21 }
22 req.user = decoded;
23 next();
24 } catch (error) {
25 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid token' });
26 }
27 }
28
29// Role-based authorization middleware
30 function requireRole(requiredRole) {
31 return (req, res, next) => {
32 if (!req.user || req.user.role !== requiredRole) {
33 return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Insufficient permissions' });
34 }
35 next();
36 };
37 }
38
39// Google authentication
40functions.http('googleAuth', async (req, res) => {
41 try {
42 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
43 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
44 }
45
46 const { idToken } = req.body;
47 if (!idToken) {
48 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'ID token is required' });
49 }
50
51 // Verify Google ID token
52 const ticket = await oauth2Client.verifyIdToken({
53 idToken,
54 audience: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
55 });
56 const payload = ticket.getPayload();
57
58 // Create JWT token for our application
59 const token = jwt.sign(
60 { userId: payload.sub,
61 email: payload.email,
62 name: payload.name || '',
63 role: 'user'
64 }, JWT_SECRET,
65 { expiresIn: '24h' }
66 );
67
68 res.json({
69 token,
70 user: {
71 id: payload.sub,
72 email: payload.email,
73 name: payload.name || ''
74 }
75 });
76 } catch (error) {
77 console.error('Google auth error:', error);
78 res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid ID token' });
79 }
80});
81
82// Protected admin endpoint
83functions.http('adminOnlyEndpoint', requireAuth, requireRole('admin'), (req, res) => {
84 res.json({
85 message: 'Admin access granted',
86 userId: req.user.userId,
87 userRole: req.user.role
88 });
89});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "fmt"
7 "net/http"
8 "strings"
9 "time"
10 "github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v4"
11 "golang.org/x/oauth2/google"
12 "google.golang.org/api/idtoken"
13)
14
15var jwtSecret = []byte("your-jwt-secret")
16const googleClientID = "your-google-client-id"
17
18type Claims struct {
19 UserID string `json:"userId"`
20 Email string `json:"email"`
21 Name string `json:"name"`
22 Role string `json:"role"`
23 jwt.RegisteredClaims
24}
25
26func requireAuth(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
27 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
28 authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
29 if authHeader == "" || !strings.HasPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ") {
30 http.Error(w, `{"error": "Missing or invalid authorization header"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
31 return
32 }
33
34 tokenString := strings.TrimPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ")
35 claims := &Claims{}
36 token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(tokenString, claims, func(token *jwt.Token) (interface{}, error) {
37 return jwtSecret, nil
38 })
39 if err != nil {
40 http.Error(w, `{"error": "Invalid token"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
41 return
42 }
43 if !token.Valid {
44 http.Error(w, `{"error": "Invalid token"}`, http.StatusUnauthorized)
45 return
46 }
47
48 ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), "user", claims)
49 next(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
50 }
51 }
52
53func requireRole(requiredRole string) func(http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
54 return func(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
55 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
56 user := r.Context().Value("user").(*Claims)
57 if user == nil || user.Role != requiredRole {
58 http.Error(w, `{"error": "Insufficient permissions"}`, http.StatusForbidden)
59 return
60 }
61 next(w, r)
62 }
63 }
64 }
65
66func GoogleAuth(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
67 if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
68 http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
69 return
70 }
71
72 var req struct {
73 IDToken string `json:"idToken"`
74 }
75 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
76 http.Error(w, "Invalid JSON", http.StatusBadRequest)
77 return
78 }
79
80 if req.IDToken == "" {
81 http.Error(w, "ID token is required", http.StatusBadRequest)
82 return
83 }
84
85 // Verify Google ID token
86 ctx := context.Background()
87 validator, err := idtoken.NewValidator(ctx, idtoken.WithAudience(googleClientID))
88 if err != nil {
89 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
90 return
91 }
92 payload, err := validator.Validate(ctx, req.IDToken, googleClientID)
93 if err != nil {
94 http.Error(w, "Invalid ID token", http.StatusUnauthorized)
95 return
96 }
97
98 // Create JWT token for our application
99 claims := Claims{
100 UserID: payload.Subject,
101 Email: payload.Claims["email"].(string),
102 Name: payload.Claims["name"].(string),
103 Role: "user",
104 RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
105 ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)),
106 },
107 }
108 token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
109 tokenString, err := token.SignedString(jwtSecret)
110 if err != nil {
111 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
112 return
113 }
114
115 response := map[string]interface{}{
116 "token": tokenString,
117 "user": map[string]string{
118 "id": payload.Subject,
119 "email": payload.Claims["email"].(string),
120 "name": payload.Claims["name"].(string),
121 },
122 }
123
124 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
125 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
126 }
127
128func AdminOnlyEndpoint(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
129 user := r.Context().Value("user").(*Claims)
130 response := map[string]string{
131 "message": "Admin access granted",
132 "userId": user.UserID,
133 "userRole": user.Role,
134 }
135 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
136 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
137 }
🔐 Security & IAM Best Practices
  • Authentication: Always validate JWT tokens and check expiration times
  • Authorization: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) for different user types
  • Input Validation: Sanitize and validate all user inputs to prevent injection attacks
  • Secrets Management: Use Secret Manager for sensitive data, never hardcode secrets
  • HTTPS Only: Always use HTTPS in production to encrypt data in transit
  • Principle of Least Privilege: Grant minimum required permissions to service accounts
💰

Pricing & Billing

Basic

Understanding Cloud Functions pricing is crucial for cost optimization. Cloud Functions pricing is based on the number of invocations, compute time, and network usage.

Cost Monitoring & Optimization
python
1import os
2import time
3from datetime import datetime, timedelta
4from google.cloud import bigquery
5from flask import jsonify
6import functions_framework
7
8 bigquery_client = bigquery.Client()
9
10def calculate_function_cost(invocations, avg_duration_ms, memory_mb, network_gb=0):
11 """Calculate estimated Cloud Functions cost."""
12 # Pricing (as of 2024, subject to change)
13 PRICE_PER_INVOCATION = 0.0000004 # $0.40 per million invocations
14 PRICE_PER_GHZ_SECOND = 0.0000100 # $0.00001 per GHz-second
15 PRICE_PER_GB_SECOND = 0.0000025 # $0.0000025 per GB-second
16 PRICE_PER_GB_EGRESS = 0.12 # $0.12 per GB egress
17
18 # Calculate costs
19 invocation_cost = invocations * PRICE_PER_INVOCATION
20 duration_seconds = avg_duration_ms / 1000.0
21 cpu_seconds = invocations * duration_seconds * 0.4 # Estimated 0.4 vCPU per function
22 memory_seconds = invocations * duration_seconds * (memory_mb / 1024.0)
23 compute_cost = (cpu_seconds * PRICE_PER_GHZ_SECOND) + (memory_seconds * PRICE_PER_GB_SECOND)
24 network_cost = network_gb * PRICE_PER_GB_EGRESS
25 total_cost = invocation_cost + compute_cost + network_cost
26
27 return {
28 'invocation_cost': round(invocation_cost, 4),
29 'compute_cost': round(compute_cost, 4),
30 'network_cost': round(network_cost, 4),
31 'total_cost': round(total_cost, 4),
32 'breakdown': {
33 'invocations': invocations,
34 'avg_duration_ms': avg_duration_ms,
35 'memory_mb': memory_mb,
36 'network_gb': network_gb
37 }
38 }
39
40@functions_framework.http
41def cost_analysis(request):
42 """Analyze Cloud Functions costs and provide optimization recommendations."""
43 if request.method != 'POST':
44 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
45
46 data = request.get_json()
47 function_name = data.get('function_name')
48 days = data.get('days', 30)
49
50 if not function_name:
51 return jsonify({'error': 'function_name is required'}), 400
52
53 try:
54 # Query billing data from BigQuery
55 query = f"""
56SELECT
57 DATE(usage_start_time) as date,
58 SUM(usage.amount) as invocations,
59 AVG(usage.amount_in_pricing_units) as avg_duration_ms,
60 SUM(cost) as total_cost
61FROM `your-project.billing.gcp_billing_export`
62WHERE
63 service.description = 'Cloud Functions'
64 AND resource.name = '{function_name}'
65 AND DATE(usage_start_time) >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL {days} DAY)
66GROUP BY date
67ORDER BY date DESC
68"""
69
70 query_job = bigquery_client.query(query)
71 results = query_job.result()
72
73 cost_data = []
74 total_invocations = 0
75 total_cost = 0.0
76
77 for row in results:
78 cost_data.append({
79 'date': row.date.isoformat(),
80 'invocations': row.invocations,
81 'avg_duration_ms': row.avg_duration_ms,
82 'cost': round(row.total_cost, 4)
83 })
84 total_invocations += row.invocations
85 total_cost += row.total_cost
86
87 # Generate optimization recommendations
88 recommendations = []
89 avg_duration = sum(row.avg_duration_ms for row in results) / len(cost_data) if cost_data else 0
90
91 if avg_duration > 5000: # More than 5 seconds
92 recommendations.append("Consider optimizing function performance - average duration is high")
93 if total_invocations < 1000000: # Less than 1M invocations
94 recommendations.append("Consider using Always Free tier for low-traffic functions")
95 if total_cost > 10.0: # More than $10
96 recommendations.append("High cost detected - review function usage and optimization opportunities")
97
98 return jsonify({
99 'function_name': function_name,
100 'period_days': days,
101 'total_invocations': total_invocations,
102 'total_cost': round(total_cost, 4),
103 'cost_data': cost_data,
104 'recommendations': recommendations
105 })
106
107except Exception as e:
108 return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500
javascript
1const {BigQuery} = require('@google-cloud/bigquery');
2const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
3
4const bigquery = new BigQuery();
5
6// Cost calculation function
7 function calculateFunctionCost(invocations, avgDurationMs, memoryMb, networkGb = 0) {
8 // Pricing (as of 2024, subject to change)
9 const PRICE_PER_INVOCATION = 0.0000004; // $0.40 per million invocations
10 const PRICE_PER_GHZ_SECOND = 0.0000100; // $0.00001 per GHz-second
11 const PRICE_PER_GB_SECOND = 0.0000025; // $0.0000025 per GB-second
12 const PRICE_PER_GB_EGRESS = 0.12; // $0.12 per GB egress
13
14 // Calculate costs
15 const invocationCost = invocations * PRICE_PER_INVOCATION;
16 const durationSeconds = avgDurationMs / 1000.0;
17 const cpuSeconds = invocations * durationSeconds * 0.4; // Estimated 0.4 vCPU per function
18 const memorySeconds = invocations * durationSeconds * (memoryMb / 1024.0);
19 const computeCost = (cpuSeconds * PRICE_PER_GHZ_SECOND) + (memorySeconds * PRICE_PER_GB_SECOND);
20 const networkCost = networkGb * PRICE_PER_GB_EGRESS;
21 const totalCost = invocationCost + computeCost + networkCost;
22
23 return {
24 invocationCost: Math.round(invocationCost * 10000) / 10000,
25 computeCost: Math.round(computeCost * 10000) / 10000,
26 networkCost: Math.round(networkCost * 10000) / 10000,
27 totalCost: Math.round(totalCost * 10000) / 10000,
28 breakdown: {
29 invocations,
30 avgDurationMs,
31 memoryMb,
32 networkGb
33 }
34 };
35 }
36
37// Cost analysis function
38functions.http('costAnalysis', async (req, res) => {
39 try {
40 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
41 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
42 }
43
44 const { functionName, days = 30 } = req.body;
45
46 if (!functionName) {
47 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'functionName is required' });
48 }
49
50 // Query billing data from BigQuery
51 const query = `
52SELECT
53 DATE(usage_start_time) as date,
54 SUM(usage.amount) as invocations,
55 AVG(usage.amount_in_pricing_units) as avg_duration_ms,
56 SUM(cost) as total_cost
57FROM \`your-project.billing.gcp_billing_export\`
58WHERE
59 service.description = 'Cloud Functions'
60 AND resource.name = '${functionName}'
61 AND DATE(usage_start_time) >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL ${days} DAY)
62GROUP BY date
63ORDER BY date DESC
64`;
65
66 const [job] = await bigquery.createQueryJob({ query });
67 const [rows] = await job.getQueryResults();
68
69 const costData = [];
70 let totalInvocations = 0;
71 let totalCost = 0.0;
72
73 rows.forEach(row => {
74 costData.push({
75 date: row.date.value,
76 invocations: row.invocations,
77 avgDurationMs: row.avg_duration_ms,
78 cost: Math.round(row.total_cost * 10000) / 10000
79 });
80 totalInvocations += row.invocations;
81 totalCost += row.total_cost;
82 });
83
84 // Generate optimization recommendations
85 const recommendations = [];
86 const avgDuration = costData.length > 0 ?
87 costData.reduce((sum, row) => sum + row.avgDurationMs, 0) / costData.length : 0;
88
89 if (avgDuration > 5000) { // More than 5 seconds
90 recommendations.push('Consider optimizing function performance - average duration is high');
91 }
92 if (totalInvocations < 1000000) { // Less than 1M invocations
93 recommendations.push('Consider using Always Free tier for low-traffic functions');
94 }
95 if (totalCost > 10.0) { // More than $10
96 recommendations.push('High cost detected - review function usage and optimization opportunities');
97 }
98
99 res.json({
100 functionName,
101 periodDays: days,
102 totalInvocations,
103 totalCost: Math.round(totalCost * 10000) / 10000,
104 costData,
105 recommendations
106 });
107 } catch (error) {
108 console.error('Cost analysis error:', error);
109 res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
110 }
111});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "fmt"
7 "net/http"
8 "time"
9 "cloud.google.com/go/bigquery"
10)
11
12type CostBreakdown struct {
13 InvocationCost float64 `json:"invocationCost"`
14 ComputeCost float64 `json:"computeCost"`
15 NetworkCost float64 `json:"networkCost"`
16 TotalCost float64 `json:"totalCost"`
17 Breakdown map[string]interface{} `json:"breakdown"`
18}
19
20func CalculateFunctionCost(invocations int64, avgDurationMs float64, memoryMb int, networkGb float64) CostBreakdown {
21 // Pricing (as of 2024, subject to change)
22 pricePerInvocation := 0.0000004 // $0.40 per million invocations
23 pricePerGhzSecond := 0.0000100 // $0.00001 per GHz-second
24 pricePerGbSecond := 0.0000025 // $0.0000025 per GB-second
25 pricePerGbEgress := 0.12 // $0.12 per GB egress
26
27 // Calculate costs
28 invocationCost := float64(invocations) * pricePerInvocation
29 durationSeconds := avgDurationMs / 1000.0
30 cpuSeconds := float64(invocations) * durationSeconds * 0.4 // Estimated 0.4 vCPU per function
31 memorySeconds := float64(invocations) * durationSeconds * (float64(memoryMb) / 1024.0)
32 computeCost := (cpuSeconds * pricePerGhzSecond) + (memorySeconds * pricePerGbSecond)
33 networkCost := networkGb * pricePerGbEgress
34 totalCost := invocationCost + computeCost + networkCost
35
36 return CostBreakdown{
37 InvocationCost: invocationCost,
38 ComputeCost: computeCost,
39 NetworkCost: networkCost,
40 TotalCost: totalCost,
41 Breakdown: map[string]interface{}{
42 "invocations": invocations,
43 "avgDurationMs": avgDurationMs,
44 "memoryMb": memoryMb,
45 "networkGb": networkGb,
46 },
47 }
48 }
49
50type CostAnalysisRequest struct {
51 FunctionName string `json:"functionName"`
52 Days int `json:"days"`
53}
54
55type CostData struct {
56 Date string `json:"date"`
57 Invocations int64 `json:"invocations"`
58 AvgDurationMs float64 `json:"avgDurationMs"`
59 Cost float64 `json:"cost"`
60}
61
62func CostAnalysis(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
63 if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
64 http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
65 return
66 }
67
68 var req CostAnalysisRequest
69 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
70 http.Error(w, "Invalid JSON", http.StatusBadRequest)
71 return
72 }
73
74 if req.Days <= 0 {
75 req.Days = 30
76 }
77
78 ctx := context.Background()
79 client, err := bigquery.NewClient(ctx, "")
80 if err != nil {
81 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
82 return
83 }
84 defer client.Close()
85
86 // Query billing data
87 query := fmt.Sprintf(`
88SELECT
89 DATE(usage_start_time) as date,
90 SUM(usage.amount) as invocations,
91 AVG(usage.amount_in_pricing_units) as avg_duration_ms,
92 SUM(cost) as total_cost
93FROM \`your-project.billing.gcp_billing_export\`
94WHERE
95 service.description = 'Cloud Functions'
96 AND resource.name = '%s'
97 AND DATE(usage_start_time) >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(), INTERVAL %d DAY)
98GROUP BY date
99ORDER BY date DESC
100`, req.FunctionName, req.Days)
101
102 q := client.Query(query)
103 it, err := q.Read(ctx)
104 if err != nil {
105 http.Error(w, "Query failed", http.StatusInternalServerError)
106 return
107 }
108
109 var costData []CostData
110 var totalInvocations int64
111 var totalCost float64
112
113 for {
114 var row map[string]bigquery.Value
115 err := it.Next(&row)
116 if err == iterator.Done {
117 break
118 }
119 if err != nil {
120 http.Error(w, "Error reading results", http.StatusInternalServerError)
121 return
122 }
123
124 date, _ := row["date"].(time.Time)
125 invocations, _ := row["invocations"].(int64)
126 avgDuration, _ := row["avg_duration_ms"].(float64)
127 cost, _ := row["total_cost"].(float64)
128
129 costData = append(costData, CostData{
130 Date: date.Format("2006-01-02"),
131 Invocations: invocations,
132 AvgDurationMs: avgDuration,
133 Cost: cost,
134 })
135 totalInvocations += invocations
136 totalCost += cost
137 }
138
139 // Generate recommendations
140 var recommendations []string
141 if len(costData) > 0 {
142 avgDuration := 0.0
143 for _, data := range costData {
144 avgDuration += data.AvgDurationMs
145 }
146 avgDuration /= float64(len(costData))
147
148 if avgDuration > 5000 {
149 recommendations = append(recommendations, "Consider optimizing function performance - average duration is high")
150 }
151 }
152 if totalInvocations < 1000000 {
153 recommendations = append(recommendations, "Consider using Always Free tier for low-traffic functions")
154 }
155 if totalCost > 10.0 {
156 recommendations = append(recommendations, "High cost detected - review function usage and optimization opportunities")
157 }
158
159 response := map[string]interface{}{
160 "functionName": req.FunctionName,
161 "periodDays": req.Days,
162 "totalInvocations": totalInvocations,
163 "totalCost": totalCost,
164 "costData": costData,
165 "recommendations": recommendations,
166 }
167
168 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
169 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
170 }
💰 Pricing & Billing Best Practices
  • Cost Monitoring: Regularly monitor function costs using Cloud Billing and BigQuery
  • Always Free Tier: Utilize the 2 million invocations free tier for low-traffic functions
  • Memory Optimization: Right-size memory allocation to balance performance and cost
  • Function Optimization: Reduce execution time to lower compute costs
  • Budget Alerts: Set up budget alerts to prevent unexpected costs
  • Cost Allocation: Use labels and projects to track costs by team or application
⚡

Performance Optimization

Advanced

Optimizing Cloud Functions performance involves reducing cold start times, minimizing execution duration, and efficiently managing resources. These techniques can significantly improve user experience and reduce costs.

Cold Start Optimization & Performance Tuning
python
1import time
2import asyncio
3import functools
4from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
5from google.cloud import firestore
6from flask import jsonify
7import functions_framework
8
9 # Global variables for connection reuse (reduces cold start time)
10db = None
11thread_pool = None
12
13def initialize_resources():
14 """Initialize resources once to reduce cold start time."""
15 global db, thread_pool
16 if db is None:
17 db = firestore.Client()
18 if thread_pool is None:
19 thread_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
20
21def performance_monitor(func):
22 """Monitor function performance."""
23 @functools.wraps(func)
24 def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
25 start_time = time.time()
26 try:
27 result = func(*args, **kwargs)
28 execution_time = time.time() - start_time
29 # Log performance metrics
30 print(f"Function {func.__name__} executed in {execution_time:.3f}s")
31 return result
32 finally:
33 pass
34 return wrapper
35
36@functions_framework.http
37@performance_monitor
38def optimized_function(request):
39 """Optimized function with connection reuse and efficient processing."""
40 # Initialize resources once
41 initialize_resources()
42
43 user_ids = request.args.getlist('user_id')
44 if not user_ids:
45 return jsonify({'error': 'user_id parameter required'}), 400
46
47 # Use thread pool for concurrent processing
48 futures = []
49 for user_id in user_ids[:5]: # Limit to 5 concurrent requests
50 future = thread_pool.submit(fetch_user_data_optimized, user_id)
51 futures.append(future)
52
53 # Collect results
54 results = []
55 for future in futures:
56 try:
57 result = future.result(timeout=10) # 10 second timeout
58 results.append(result)
59 except Exception as e:
60 results.append({'error': str(e)})
61
62 return jsonify({
63 'results': results,
64 'processed_count': len(results),
65 'optimization': 'connection_reuse_thread_pool'
66 })
67
68def fetch_user_data_optimized(user_id):
69 """Fetch user data using reused connection."""
70 # Use global db connection instead of creating new one
71 doc = db.collection('users').document(user_id).get()
72 if doc.exists:
73 data = doc.to_dict()
74 return {
75 'user_id': user_id,
76 'name': data.get('name'),
77 'email': data.get('email'),
78 'status': 'found'
79 }
80 else:
81 return {
82 'user_id': user_id,
83 'status': 'not_found'
84 }
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
3
4// Global connections for reuse
5let db;
6
7// Initialize resources once
8 function initializeResources() {
9 if (!db) {
10 db = new Firestore();
11 }
12 }
13
14// Performance monitoring middleware
15 function performanceMonitor(req, res, next) {
16 const startTime = process.hrtime.bigint();
17 const originalEnd = res.end;
18 res.end = function(...args) {
19 const endTime = process.hrtime.bigint();
20 const executionTime = Number(endTime - startTime) / 1e6; // Convert to milliseconds
21 console.log(`Function executed in ${executionTime.toFixed(2)}ms`);
22 originalEnd.apply(this, args);
23 };
24 next();
25 }
26
27// Optimized function with connection reuse
28functions.http('optimizedFunction', performanceMonitor, async (req, res) => {
29 try {
30 // Initialize resources once
31 initializeResources();
32
33 const userIds = req.query.userId;
34 if (!userIds) {
35 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'userId parameter required' });
36 }
37
38 const userIdArray = Array.isArray(userIds) ? userIds : [userIds];
39 const limitedUserIds = userIdArray.slice(0, 5); // Limit concurrency
40
41 // Process concurrently with Promise.allSettled
42 const promises = limitedUserIds.map(userId => fetchUserDataOptimized(userId));
43 const results = await Promise.allSettled(promises);
44
45 const processedResults = results.map((result, index) => {
46 if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
47 return result.value;
48 } else {
49 return { userId: limitedUserIds[index], error: result.reason.message };
50 }
51 });
52
53 res.json({
54 results: processedResults,
55 processedCount: processedResults.length,
56 optimization: 'connectionReuse'
57 });
58 } catch (error) {
59 console.error('Optimized function error:', error);
60 res.status(500).json({ error: error.message });
61 }
62});
63
64// Optimized user data fetching
65 async function fetchUserDataOptimized(userId) {
66 // Use global db connection
67 const doc = await db.collection('users').doc(userId).get();
68 if (doc.exists) {
69 const data = doc.data();
70 return {
71 userId,
72 name: data.name,
73 email: data.email,
74 status: 'found'
75 };
76 } else {
77 return {
78 userId,
79 status: 'not_found'
80 };
81 }
82 }
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "log"
7 "net/http"
8 "sync"
9 "time"
10 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
11)
12
13var (
14 db *firestore.Client
15 dbOnce sync.Once
16 )
17
18func initDB() {
19 dbOnce.Do(func() {
20 ctx := context.Background()
21 client, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, "")
22 if err != nil {
23 log.Printf("Failed to create Firestore client: %v", err)
24 return
25 }
26 db = client
27 })
28 }
29
30type UserResult struct {
31 UserID string `json:"userId"`
32 Name string `json:"name,omitempty"`
33 Email string `json:"email,omitempty"`
34 Status string `json:"status"`
35 Error string `json:"error,omitempty"`
36}
37
38func performanceMonitor(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
39 return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
40 start := time.Now()
41 // Wrap ResponseWriter to capture completion
42 wrapped := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w}
43 next(wrapped, r)
44 duration := time.Since(start)
45 log.Printf("Function executed in %v", duration)
46 }
47 }
48
49type responseWriter struct {
50 http.ResponseWriter
51 }
52
53func OptimizedFunction(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
54 // Initialize DB connection once
55 initDB()
56
57 userIDs := r.URL.Query()["userId"]
58 if len(userIDs) == 0 {
59 http.Error(w, `{"error": "userId parameter required"}`, http.StatusBadRequest)
60 return
61 }
62
63 // Limit concurrency to prevent resource exhaustion
64 maxConcurrent := 5
65 if len(userIDs) > maxConcurrent {
66 userIDs = userIDs[:maxConcurrent]
67 }
68
69 ctx := context.Background()
70 semaphore := make(chan struct{}, maxConcurrent)
71 resultsChan := make(chan UserResult, len(userIDs))
72
73 // Start goroutines with semaphore control
74 var wg sync.WaitGroup
75 for _, userID := range userIDs {
76 wg.Add(1)
77 go func(uid string) {
78 defer wg.Done()
79 semaphore <- struct{}{} // Acquire
80 defer func() { <-semaphore }() // Release
81
82 result := fetchUserDataOptimized(ctx, uid)
83 resultsChan <- result
84 }(userID)
85 }
86
87 // Close channel when all goroutines complete
88 go func() {
89 wg.Wait()
90 close(resultsChan)
91 }()
92
93 // Collect results
94 var results []UserResult
95 for result := range resultsChan {
96 results = append(results, result)
97 }
98
99 response := map[string]interface{}{
100 "results": results,
101 "processedCount": len(results),
102 "optimization": "connectionReuse_semaphore",
103 }
104
105 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
106 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(response)
107 }
108
109func fetchUserDataOptimized(ctx context.Context, userID string) UserResult {
110 // Use global DB connection
111 doc, err := db.Collection("users").Doc(userID).Get(ctx)
112 if err != nil {
113 return UserResult{
114 UserID: userID,
115 Status: "error",
116 Error: err.Error(),
117 }
118 }
119
120 if !doc.Exists() {
121 return UserResult{
122 UserID: userID,
123 Status: "not_found",
124 }
125 }
126
127 var data map[string]interface{}
128 if err := doc.DataTo(&data); err != nil {
129 return UserResult{
130 UserID: userID,
131 Status: "error",
132 Error: err.Error(),
133 }
134 }
135
136 return UserResult{
137 UserID: userID,
138 Name: data["name"].(string),
139 Email: data["email"].(string),
140 Status: "found",
141 }
142 }
⚡ Performance Optimization Best Practices
  • Connection Reuse: Initialize database connections once and reuse them across function invocations
  • Minimize Cold Starts: Keep functions warm with Cloud Scheduler or keep warm functions
  • Memory Allocation: Right-size memory allocation for optimal CPU performance
  • Concurrent Processing: Use appropriate concurrency limits to prevent resource exhaustion
  • Efficient Algorithms: Optimize code for speed and memory usage
  • Caching: Implement caching for frequently accessed data
🚀

Real-world Project Examples

Advanced

Explore comprehensive real-world implementations of Cloud Functions across various use cases, from web applications to machine learning pipelines. These examples demonstrate production-ready patterns with proper error handling, monitoring, and scalability considerations.

Web Application: User Authentication & Profile Management
python
1import functions_framework
2from flask import request, jsonify
3from google.cloud import firestore
4from google.auth.transport import requests
5import jwt
6import bcrypt
7import logging
8from datetime import datetime, timedelta
9
10db = firestore.Client()
11JWT_SECRET = 'your-jwt-secret'
12logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
13
14@functions_framework.http
15def register_user(request):
16 """Register a new user with email and password."""
17 if request.method != 'POST':
18 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
19
20 data = request.get_json()
21 email = data.get('email')
22 password = data.get('password')
23 name = data.get('name')
24
25 if not all([email, password, name]):
26 return jsonify({'error': 'Email, password, and name are required'}), 400
27
28 try:
29 # Check if user already exists
30 user_ref = db.collection('users').document(email)
31 if user_ref.get().exists:
32 return jsonify({'error': 'User already exists'}), 409
33
34 # Hash password
35 password_hash = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode('utf-8'), bcrypt.gensalt()).decode('utf-8')
36
37 # Create user document
38 user_data = {
39 'email': email,
40 'name': name,
41 'password_hash': password_hash,
42 'created_at': datetime.utcnow(),
43 'last_login': None,
44 'is_active': True
45 }
46 user_ref.set(user_data)
47
48 logger.info(f"User registered: {email}")
49 return jsonify({'message': 'User registered successfully'}), 201
50
51except Exception as e:
52 logger.error(f"Registration failed: {str(e)}")
53 return jsonify({'error': 'Internal server error'}), 500
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
3const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
4const bcrypt = require('bcrypt');
5
6const db = new Firestore();
7const JWT_SECRET = 'your-jwt-secret';
8
9// User registration function
10functions.http('registerUser', async (req, res) => {
11 try {
12 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
13 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
14 }
15
16 const { email, password, name } = req.body;
17
18 if (!email || !password || !name) {
19 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Email, password, and name are required' });
20 }
21
22 // Check if user exists
23 const userRef = db.collection('users').doc(email);
24 const userDoc = await userRef.get();
25 if (userDoc.exists) {
26 return res.status(409).json({ error: 'User already exists' });
27 }
28
29 // Hash password
30 const saltRounds = 10;
31 const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, saltRounds);
32
33 // Create user
34 await userRef.set({
35 email,
36 name,
37 passwordHash,
38 createdAt: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp(),
39 lastLogin: null,
40 isActive: true
41 });
42
43 console.log(`User registered: ${email}`);
44 res.status(201).json({ message: 'User registered successfully' });
45 } catch (error) {
46 console.error('Registration error:', error);
47 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
48 }
49});
50
51// User login function
52functions.http('loginUser', async (req, res) => {
53 try {
54 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
55 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
56 }
57
58 const { email, password } = req.body;
59
60 if (!email || !password) {
61 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Email and password are required' });
62 }
63
64 // Get user
65 const userRef = db.collection('users').doc(email);
66 const userDoc = await userRef.get();
67 if (!userDoc.exists) {
68 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' });
69 }
70
71 const userData = userDoc.data();
72 if (!userData.isActive) {
73 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Account is disabled' });
74 }
75
76 // Verify password
77 const isValidPassword = await bcrypt.compare(password, userData.passwordHash);
78 if (!isValidPassword) {
79 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' });
80 }
81
82 // Generate JWT
83 const token = jwt.sign(
84 { email: userData.email,
85 name: userData.name
86 }, JWT_SECRET,
87 { expiresIn: '24h' }
88 );
89
90 // Update last login
91 await userRef.update({
92 lastLogin: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp()
93 });
94
95 console.log(`User logged in: ${email}`);
96 res.json({
97 token,
98 user: {
99 email: userData.email,
100 name: userData.name
101 }
102 });
103 } catch (error) {
104 console.error('Login error:', error);
105 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
106 }
107});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "log"
7 "net/http"
8 "time"
9 "golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
10 "github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v4"
11 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
12)
13
14var jwtSecret = []byte("your-jwt-secret")
15
16type User struct {
17 Email string `json:"email" firestore:"email"`
18 Name string `json:"name" firestore:"name"`
19 PasswordHash string `json:"-" firestore:"passwordHash"`
20 CreatedAt time.Time `json:"createdAt" firestore:"createdAt"`
21 LastLogin *time.Time `json:"lastLogin" firestore:"lastLogin"`
22 IsActive bool `json:"isActive" firestore:"isActive"`
23}
24
25type RegisterRequest struct {
26 Email string `json:"email"`
27 Password string `json:"password"`
28 Name string `json:"name"`
29}
30
31func RegisterUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
32 if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
33 http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
34 return
35 }
36
37 var req RegisterRequest
38 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
39 http.Error(w, "Invalid JSON", http.StatusBadRequest)
40 return
41 }
42
43 if req.Email == "" || req.Password == "" || req.Name == "" {
44 http.Error(w, "Email, password, and name are required", http.StatusBadRequest)
45 return
46 }
47
48 ctx := context.Background()
49 client, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, "")
50 if err != nil {
51 log.Printf("Failed to create Firestore client: %v", err)
52 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
53 return
54 }
55 defer client.Close()
56
57 // Check if user exists
58 userRef := client.Collection("users").Doc(req.Email)
59 _, err = userRef.Get(ctx)
60 if err == nil {
61 http.Error(w, "User already exists", http.StatusConflict)
62 return
63 }
64
65 // Hash password
66 passwordHash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(req.Password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
67 if err != nil {
68 log.Printf("Failed to hash password: %v", err)
69 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
70 return
71 }
72
73 // Create user
74 user := User{
75 Email: req.Email,
76 Name: req.Name,
77 PasswordHash: string(passwordHash),
78 CreatedAt: time.Now(),
79 IsActive: true,
80 }
81 _, err = userRef.Set(ctx, user)
82 if err != nil {
83 log.Printf("Failed to create user: %v", err)
84 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
85 return
86 }
87
88 log.Printf("User registered: %s", req.Email)
89 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
90 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"message": "User registered successfully"})
91}
🔐 Authentication Best Practices
  • Password Security: Use bcrypt for hashing with appropriate cost factor
  • JWT Tokens: Set reasonable expiration times and store securely
  • Input Validation: Validate all user inputs and sanitize data
  • Error Handling: Don't expose sensitive information in error messages
  • Rate Limiting: Implement rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks
  • Session Management: Track user sessions and handle logout properly
☁️

Cloud Events & Eventarc

Advanced

Cloud Events and Eventarc enable event-driven architectures by routing events from Google Cloud services to Cloud Functions. This creates loosely coupled, scalable systems that react to changes in your infrastructure and data.

⚡ Event-Driven Function Flow: Event Source → Eventarc → Function → Action
📡 Event Source
Cloud Storage, Firestore, etc.
🎯 Eventarc
Event Routing
⚡ Function
Event Handler
🔄 Action
Process & Respond
Event-Driven Serverless Architecture
Cloud Storage Event Handler
python
1import json
2import base64
3from google.cloud import storage, firestore
4from google.events.cloud.storage import StorageObjectData
5from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent
6
7storage_client = storage.Client()
8firestore_client = firestore.Client()
9
10def process_image_upload(cloudevent):
11 """Process image uploads to Cloud Storage using Cloud Events."""
12 try:
13 # Parse Cloud Event
14 event = CloudEvent(cloudevent)
15 event_data = event.data
16
17 bucket_name = event_data['bucket']
18 file_name = event_data['name']
19 content_type = event_data.get('contentType', '')
20
21 # Only process image files
22 if not content_type.startswith('image/'):
23 return f"Skipped non-image file: {file_name}"
24
25 # Get file metadata
26 bucket = storage_client.bucket(bucket_name)
27 blob = bucket.blob(file_name)
28 metadata = blob.metadata or {}
29 size = blob.size
30
31 # Store metadata in Firestore
32 doc_ref = firestore_client.collection('images').document(file_name)
33 doc_ref.set({
34 'bucket': bucket_name,
35 'file_name': file_name,
36 'content_type': content_type,
37 'size': size,
38 'uploaded_at': firestore.SERVER_TIMESTAMP,
39 'metadata': metadata,
40 'processed': False
41 })
42
43 return f"Processed image: {file_name}, size: {size} bytes"
44
45 except Exception as e:
46 return f"Error processing {file_name}: {str(e)}"
javascript
1const {Storage} = require('@google-cloud/storage');
2const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
3const {CloudEvent} = require('cloudevents');
4
5const storage = new Storage();
6const firestore = new Firestore();
7
8// Cloud Storage event handler
9exports.processImageUpload = (cloudevent) => {
10 try {
11 // Parse Cloud Event
12 const event = new CloudEvent(cloudevent);
13 const eventData = event.data;
14
15 const bucketName = eventData.bucket;
16 const fileName = eventData.name;
17 const contentType = eventData.contentType || '';
18
19 // Only process image files
20 if (!contentType.startsWith('image/')) {
21 return `Skipped non-image file: ${fileName}`;
22 }
23
24 // Get file metadata
25 const bucket = storage.bucket(bucketName);
26 const file = bucket.file(fileName);
27
28 const [metadata] = await file.getMetadata();
29 const size = metadata.size;
30
31 // Store metadata in Firestore
32 const docRef = firestore.collection('images').doc(fileName);
33 await docRef.set({
34 bucket: bucketName,
35 fileName: fileName,
36 contentType: contentType,
37 size: size,
38 uploadedAt: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp(),
39 metadata: metadata.metadata || {},
40 processed: false
41 });
42
43 return `Processed image: ${fileName}, size: ${size} bytes`;
44
45 } catch (error) {
46 return `Error processing ${fileName}: ${error.message}`;
47 }
48};
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "fmt"
7 "log"
8 "strings"
9 "time"
10
11 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
12 "cloud.google.com/go/storage"
13 "github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/event"
14 "github.com/google/uuid"
15)
16
17// ProcessImageUpload handles Cloud Storage events
18func ProcessImageUpload(ctx context.Context, e event.Event) error {
19 var eventData map[string]interface{}
20 if err := e.DataAs(&eventData); err != nil {
21 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event data: %v", err)
22 }
23
24 bucketName, ok := eventData["bucket"].(string)
25 if !ok {
26 return fmt.Errorf("bucket name not found in event")
27 }
28 fileName, ok := eventData["name"].(string)
29 if !ok {
30 return fmt.Errorf("file name not found in event")
31 }
32 contentType, _ := eventData["contentType"].(string)
33
34 // Only process image files
35 if !strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "image/") {
36 log.Printf("Skipped non-image file: %s", fileName)
37 return nil
38 }
39
40 // Initialize clients
41 storageClient, err := storage.NewClient(ctx)
42 if err != nil {
43 return fmt.Errorf("failed to create storage client: %v", err)
44 }
45 defer storageClient.Close()
46
47 firestoreClient, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, firestore.DetectProjectID)
48 if err != nil {
49 return fmt.Errorf("failed to create firestore client: %v", err)
50 }
51 defer firestoreClient.Close()
52
53 // Get file metadata
54 bucket := storageClient.Bucket(bucketName)
55 obj := bucket.Object(fileName)
56 attrs, err := obj.Attrs(ctx)
57 if err != nil {
58 return fmt.Errorf("failed to get object attributes: %v", err)
59 }
60
61 // Store metadata in Firestore
62 docRef := firestoreClient.Collection("images").Doc(fileName)
63 _, err = docRef.Set(ctx, map[string]interface{}{
64 "bucket": bucketName,
65 "fileName": fileName,
66 "contentType": contentType,
67 "size": attrs.Size,
68 "uploadedAt": firestore.ServerTimestamp,
69 "metadata": attrs.Metadata,
70 "processed": false,
71 })
72 if err != nil {
73 return fmt.Errorf("failed to store metadata: %v", err)
74 }
75
76 log.Printf("Processed image: %s, size: %d bytes", fileName, attrs.Size)
77 return nil
78}
Firestore Event Handler
python
1import json
2from google.cloud import firestore
3from google.events.cloud.firestore import DocumentEventData
4from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent
5
6firestore_client = firestore.Client()
7
8def sync_user_profile(cloudevent):
9 """Sync user profile changes to search index using Cloud Events."""
10 try:
11 # Parse Cloud Event
12 event = CloudEvent(cloudevent)
13 event_data = event.data
14
15 # Extract document information
16 document_path = event_data['value']['name']
17 document_data = event_data['value']['fields']
18
19 # Parse document path to get user ID
20 path_parts = document_path.split('/')
21 user_id = path_parts[-1] # Last part is document ID
22
23 # Extract user data
24 user_data = {
25 'user_id': user_id,
26 'name': document_data.get('name', {}).get('stringValue', ''),
27 'email': document_data.get('email', {}).get('stringValue', ''),
28 'updated_at': firestore.SERVER_TIMESTAMP
29 }
30
31 # Update search index collection
32 search_ref = firestore_client.collection('user_search').document(user_id)
33 search_ref.set(user_data)
34
35 # Log activity
36 activity_ref = firestore_client.collection('user_activity').document()
37 activity_ref.set({
38 'user_id': user_id,
39 'action': 'profile_updated',
40 'timestamp': firestore.SERVER_TIMESTAMP,
41 'source': 'firestore_event'
42 })
43
44 return f"Profile synced for user: {user_id}"
45
46 except Exception as e:
47 return f"Error syncing profile: {str(e)}"
javascript
1const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
2const {CloudEvent} = require('cloudevents');
3
const firestore = new Firestore();
4
5// Firestore event handler
6exports.syncUserProfile = (cloudevent) => {
7 try {
8 // Parse Cloud Event
9 const event = new CloudEvent(cloudevent);
10 const eventData = event.data;
11
12 // Extract document information
13 const documentPath = eventData.value.name;
14 const documentData = eventData.value.fields;
15
16 // Parse document path to get user ID
17 const pathParts = documentPath.split('/');
18 const userId = pathParts[pathParts.length - 1]; // Last part is document ID
19
20 // Extract user data
21 const userData = {
22 userId: userId,
23 name: documentData.name?.stringValue || '',
24 email: documentData.email?.stringValue || '',
25 updatedAt: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp()
26 };
27
28 // Update search index collection
29 const searchRef = firestore.collection('user_search').doc(userId);
30 await searchRef.set(userData);
31
32 // Log activity
33 const activityRef = firestore.collection('user_activity').doc();
34 await activityRef.set({
35 userId: userId,
36 action: 'profile_updated',
37 timestamp: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp(),
38 source: 'firestore_event'
39 });
40
41 return `Profile synced for user: ${userId}`;
42
43 } catch (error) {
44 return `Error syncing profile: ${error.message}`;
45 }
46};
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "fmt"
7 "log"
8 "strings"
9
"cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
10 "github.com/cloudevents/sdk-go/v2/event"
11)
12
13// SyncUserProfile handles Firestore document change events
14func SyncUserProfile(ctx context.Context, e event.Event) error {
15 var eventData map[string]interface{}
16 if err := e.DataAs(&eventData); err != nil {
17 return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse event data: %v", err)
18 }
19
20 // Extract document information
21 value, ok := eventData["value"].(map[string]interface{})
22 if !ok {
23 return fmt.Errorf("value not found in event")
24 }
25
26 documentPath, ok := value["name"].(string)
27 if !ok {
28 return fmt.Errorf("document path not found in event")
29 }
30
31 fields, ok := value["fields"].(map[string]interface{})
32 if !ok {
33 return fmt.Errorf("fields not found in event")
34 }
35
36 // Parse document path to get user ID
37 pathParts := strings.Split(documentPath, "/")
38 userID := pathParts[len(pathParts)-1] // Last part is document ID
39
// Extract user data
40 userData := map[string]interface{}{
41 "userId": userID,
42 "updatedAt": firestore.ServerTimestamp,
43 }
44
45 // Extract name and email from fields
46 if nameField, ok := fields["name"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
47 if nameValue, ok := nameField["stringValue"].(string); ok {
48 userData["name"] = nameValue
49 }
50 }
51 if emailField, ok := fields["email"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
52 if emailValue, ok := emailField["stringValue"].(string); ok {
53 userData["email"] = emailValue
54 }
55 }
56
57 // Initialize Firestore client
58 firestoreClient, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, firestore.DetectProjectID)
59 if err != nil {
60 return fmt.Errorf("failed to create firestore client: %v", err)
61 }
62 defer firestoreClient.Close()
63
64 // Update search index collection
65 searchRef := firestoreClient.Collection("user_search").Doc(userID)
66 _, err = searchRef.Set(ctx, userData)
67 if err != nil {
68 return fmt.Errorf("failed to update search index: %v", err)
69 }
70
71 // Log activity
72 activityRef := firestoreClient.Collection("user_activity").NewDoc()
73 _, err = activityRef.Set(ctx, map[string]interface{}{
74 "userId": userID,
75 "action": "profile_updated",
76 "timestamp": firestore.ServerTimestamp,
77 "source": "firestore_event",
78 })
79 if err != nil {
80 return fmt.Errorf("failed to log activity: %v", err)
81 }
82
83 log.Printf("Profile synced for user: %s", userID)
84 return nil
85}
☁️ Cloud Events & Eventarc Best Practices
  • Event Filtering: Use Eventarc filters to reduce function invocations and costs
  • Idempotency: Design functions to handle duplicate events gracefully
  • Error Handling: Implement proper error handling and dead letter queues
  • Event Ordering: Don't assume events arrive in order; use sequence tokens when needed
  • Performance: Keep event processing lightweight; offload heavy work to background tasks
  • Security: Validate event sources and use service accounts with minimal permissions
🚀

Real-world Project Examples

Advanced

Explore comprehensive real-world implementations of Cloud Functions across various use cases, from web applications to machine learning pipelines. These examples demonstrate production-ready patterns with proper error handling, monitoring, and scalability considerations.

Web Application: User Authentication & Profile Management
python
1import functions_framework
2from flask import request, jsonify
3from google.cloud import firestore
4from google.auth.transport import requests
5import jwt
6import bcrypt
7import logging
8from datetime import datetime, timedelta
9
10db = firestore.Client()
11JWT_SECRET = 'your-jwt-secret'
12logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
13
14@functions_framework.http
15def register_user(request):
16 """Register a new user with email and password."""
17 if request.method != 'POST':
18 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
19
20 data = request.get_json()
21 email = data.get('email')
22 password = data.get('password')
23 name = data.get('name')
24
25 if not all([email, password, name]):
26 return jsonify({'error': 'Email, password, and name are required'}), 400
27
28 try:
29 # Check if user already exists
30 user_ref = db.collection('users').document(email)
31 if user_ref.get().exists:
32 return jsonify({'error': 'User already exists'}), 409
33
34 # Hash password
35 password_hash = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode('utf-8'), bcrypt.gensalt()).decode('utf-8')
36
37 # Create user document
38 user_data = {
39 'email': email,
40 'name': name,
41 'password_hash': password_hash,
42 'created_at': datetime.utcnow(),
43 'last_login': None,
44 'is_active': True
45 }
46 user_ref.set(user_data)
47
48 logger.info(f"User registered: {email}")
49 return jsonify({'message': 'User registered successfully'}), 201
50
51except Exception as e:
52 logger.error(f"Registration failed: {str(e)}")
53 return jsonify({'error': 'Internal server error'}), 500
54
55@functions_framework.http
56def login_user(request):
57 """Authenticate user and return JWT token."""
58 if request.method != 'POST':
59 return jsonify({'error': 'Method not allowed'}), 405
60
61 data = request.get_json()
62 email = data.get('email')
63 password = data.get('password')
64
65 if not all([email, password]):
66 return jsonify({'error': 'Email and password are required'}), 400
67
68 try:
69 user_doc = db.collection('users').document(email).get()
70 if not user_doc.exists:
71 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid credentials'}), 401
72
73 user_data = user_doc.to_dict()
74 if not user_data.get('is_active', False):
75 return jsonify({'error': 'Account is disabled'}), 401
76
77 # Verify password
78 if not bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode('utf-8'), user_data['password_hash'].encode('utf-8')):
79 return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid credentials'}), 401
80
81 # Generate JWT token
82 payload = {
83 'email': email,
84 'name': user_data['name'],
85 'exp': datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=24)
86 }
87 token = jwt.encode(payload, JWT_SECRET, algorithm='HS256')
88
89 # Update last login
90 db.collection('users').document(email).update({
91 'last_login': datetime.utcnow()
92 })
93
94 logger.info(f"User logged in: {email}")
95 return jsonify({
96 'token': token,
97 'user': {
98 'email': email,
99 'name': user_data['name']
100 }
101 }), 200
102
103except Exception as e:
104 logger.error(f"Login failed: {str(e)}")
105 return jsonify({'error': 'Internal server error'}), 500
javascript
1const functions = require('@google-cloud/functions-framework');
2const {Firestore} = require('@google-cloud/firestore');
3const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
4const bcrypt = require('bcrypt');
5
6const db = new Firestore();
7const JWT_SECRET = 'your-jwt-secret';
8
9// User registration function
10functions.http('registerUser', async (req, res) => {
11 try {
12 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
13 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
14 }
15
16 const { email, password, name } = req.body;
17
18 if (!email || !password || !name) {
19 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Email, password, and name are required' });
20 }
21
22 // Check if user exists
23 const userRef = db.collection('users').doc(email);
24 const userDoc = await userRef.get();
25 if (userDoc.exists) {
26 return res.status(409).json({ error: 'User already exists' });
27 }
28
29 // Hash password
30 const saltRounds = 10;
31 const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, saltRounds);
32
33 // Create user
34 await userRef.set({
35 email,
36 name,
37 passwordHash,
38 createdAt: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp(),
39 lastLogin: null,
40 isActive: true
41 });
42
43 console.log(`User registered: ${email}`);
44 res.status(201).json({ message: 'User registered successfully' });
45 } catch (error) {
46 console.error('Registration error:', error);
47 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
48 }
49});
50
51// User login function
52functions.http('loginUser', async (req, res) => {
53 try {
54 if (req.method !== 'POST') {
55 return res.status(405).json({ error: 'Method not allowed' });
56 }
57
58 const { email, password } = req.body;
59
60 if (!email || !password) {
61 return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Email and password are required' });
62 }
63
64 // Get user
65 const userRef = db.collection('users').doc(email);
66 const userDoc = await userRef.get();
67 if (!userDoc.exists) {
68 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' });
69 }
70
71 const userData = userDoc.data();
72 if (!userData.isActive) {
73 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Account is disabled' });
74 }
75
76 // Verify password
77 const isValidPassword = await bcrypt.compare(password, userData.passwordHash);
78 if (!isValidPassword) {
79 return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Invalid credentials' });
80 }
81
82 // Generate JWT
83 const token = jwt.sign(
84 { email: userData.email,
85 name: userData.name
86 }, JWT_SECRET,
87 { expiresIn: '24h' }
88 );
89
90 // Update last login
91 await userRef.update({
92 lastLogin: Firestore.FieldValue.serverTimestamp()
93 });
94
95 console.log(`User logged in: ${email}`);
96 res.json({
97 token,
98 user: {
99 email: userData.email,
100 name: userData.name
101 }
102 });
103 } catch (error) {
104 console.error('Login error:', error);
105 res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
106 }
107});
go
1package function
2
3import (
4 "context"
5 "encoding/json"
6 "log"
7 "net/http"
8 "time"
9 "golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
10 "github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v4"
11 "cloud.google.com/go/firestore"
12)
13
14var jwtSecret = []byte("your-jwt-secret")
15
16type User struct {
17 Email string `json:"email" firestore:"email"`
18 Name string `json:"name" firestore:"name"`
19 PasswordHash string `json:"-" firestore:"passwordHash"`
20 CreatedAt time.Time `json:"createdAt" firestore:"createdAt"`
21 LastLogin *time.Time `json:"lastLogin" firestore:"lastLogin"`
22 IsActive bool `json:"isActive" firestore:"isActive"`
23}
24
25type RegisterRequest struct {
26 Email string `json:"email"`
27 Password string `json:"password"`
28 Name string `json:"name"`
29}
30
31func RegisterUser(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
32 if r.Method != http.MethodPost {
33 http.Error(w, "Method not allowed", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
34 return
35 }
36
37 var req RegisterRequest
38 if err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {
39 http.Error(w, "Invalid JSON", http.StatusBadRequest)
40 return
41 }
42
43 if req.Email == "" || req.Password == "" || req.Name == "" {
44 http.Error(w, "Email, password, and name are required", http.StatusBadRequest)
45 return
46 }
47
48 ctx := context.Background()
49 client, err := firestore.NewClient(ctx, "")
50 if err != nil {
51 log.Printf("Failed to create Firestore client: %v", err)
52 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
53 return
54 }
55 defer client.Close()
56
57 // Check if user exists
58 userRef := client.Collection("users").Doc(req.Email)
59 _, err = userRef.Get(ctx)
60 if err == nil {
61 http.Error(w, "User already exists", http.StatusConflict)
62 return
63 }
64
65 // Hash password
66 passwordHash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(req.Password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
67 if err != nil {
68 log.Printf("Failed to hash password: %v", err)
69 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
70 return
71 }
72
73 // Create user
74 user := User{
75 Email: req.Email,
76 Name: req.Name,
77 PasswordHash: string(passwordHash),
78 CreatedAt: time.Now(),
79 IsActive: true,
80 }
81 _, err = userRef.Set(ctx, user)
82 if err != nil {
83 log.Printf("Failed to create user: %v", err)
84 http.Error(w, "Internal server error", http.StatusInternalServerError)
85 return
86 }
87
88 log.Printf("User registered: %s", req.Email)
89 w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
90 json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{"message": "User registered successfully"})
91}
🔐 Authentication Best Practices
  • Password Security: Use bcrypt for hashing with appropriate cost factor
  • JWT Tokens: Set reasonable expiration times and store securely
  • Input Validation: Validate all user inputs and sanitize data
  • Error Handling: Don't expose sensitive information in error messages
  • Rate Limiting: Implement rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks
  • Session Management: Track user sessions and handle logout properly
